poem0096_qa.text_ar: وكل شاعر قانص وكلً يدسم شاربه مخلابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: Every poet is a hunter and each anoints his mustache with his prey
One whose imagination is free and one who is a hireling
And someone whose imagination is like a nightmare in a train

poem0096_qa.text_ar: وأنا خيالي كل ما هديته حوّل على الوادي وصاد عقابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: And my imagination, whenever I calmed it, turned to the valley and hunted its eagle
I participated to represent my homeland and my territories
And each one represents his homeland and his relatives

poem0096_qa.text_ar: والله إذا الله راد والله قاله
poem0096_qa.translation_en: By God, if God wills it, by God He has said it
I shall hand over to her the pride and its book
My homeland is Qatar, and I am here representing it
Glory is what I seek and I am its seeker

poem0096_qa.text_ar: عهدٍ علي إني لا أشرّف داري، ترّك صبي ما يعزّ ترابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: I vow that I will not honor my home, leaving a boy who does not cherish its soil

poem0096_qa.text_ar: عهدٍ علي إني لا أشرّف داري، ترّك صبي ما يعزّ ترابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: I vow that I will not honor my home, leaving a boy who does not cherish its soil

poem0096_qa.text_ar: شوفوا دمي من حبها عنابي وحماستي من ضوها شبابة
poem0096_qa.translation_en: Look at my blood turned crimson from her love, and my passion from her light is youthful

poem0096_qa.text_ar: شاعر خليجي والعروبة بيتي والبيت ما يوقف بدون أطنابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: I am a Gulf poet and Arabism is my home, and a home cannot stand without its tent ropes
And if you return as a source of support among the tribes

poem0096_qa.text_ar: أنا أشهد إن لي في اللوازم لابة
poem0096_qa.translation_en: I bear witness that I have a need in necessities
I have allies among the tribes and I value them
I am not one who ignores the status of his companions

poem0096_qa.text_ar: وإن عوّدت لأصلي فأنا أصلي مرّي
poem0096_qa.translation_en: And if you return to my origin, then I am my origin's bitter one

poem0096_qa.text_ar: من صلب يام أهل القنا وخضابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: From the lineage of those who wield spears and their blood-staining

poem0096_qa.text_ar: قومٍ لنا فوق المعالي بيرق والسيف قوة سلته بنصابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: A people for whom we have a banner above the heights, and the sword is a force drawn from its hilt
Poetry has embraced me and I have embraced it

poem0096_qa.text_ar: قمت أتمشى به و أفك أبوابه
poem0096_qa.translation_en: I started walking with it and unlocking its doors
O girl, you who distinguish yourself with your fragrance
I don't blame them if they speak at length

poem0096_qa.text_ar: لا مرّ بش دربش على خلق الله
poem0096_qa.translation_en: When he passes by on his path among God's creation
They said thus with the index finger, how beautiful
His grandfather's and father's deeds adorn him
And you, in your turn, are a seeker/earner of purity
My people boast about me while I lower my head
Upon you is the cloak of honor's veil

poem0096_qa.text_ar: وإن ما سترتش بالفعول و باسمي
poem0096_qa.translation_en: And if you did not cover yourself with deeds and my name
So pass me the coffee cup, O coffee pourer

poem0093_qa.text_ar: يا هيه التفت لي واسمع دعاي لك، يا هيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Oh you, turn to me and hear my plea to you, oh you

poem0093_qa.text_ar: ترى عادك بتشبع من البعد وتواجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Will you ever have enough of distance and face me?

poem0093_qa.text_ar: دعيتك بصوت ياصلك، ليه ما توحيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: I called you with a voice that reaches you, why don't you respond? And I hear your ribs echoing insistently from the sound

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وانا اسمع ضلوعك من صدى الصوت لجاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Your heart trembles, its keeper is listening intently. Fear God regarding its keeper, don't you hear the trembling?

poem0093_qa.text_ar: عليك الفواد خلاجه مصمخٍ راعيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Forgiveness has come to you, seize its beginning and benefit from what follows. And know that patience is an ocean, but don't trust its waves

poem0093_qa.text_ar: خف الله في راعيه ما تسمع خلاجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Love is a dark night, I came to you as a traveler. Your connection is its lamp, and estrangement blows out its lamp

poem0093_qa.text_ar: ترى العفو قد جاك أوله واغتنم تاليه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Our palace that we've been building for years, the wind of blame has knocked down its doors and fences

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وترى الحلم بحر، غير لا تامن مواجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: For days I don't know where your home is or why. Yet I know I am the door to your longing and its bolt

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وترى الود ليل مظلم جيت لك ساريه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Absence without reason, departure without warning. The estrangement of the dearest Arabs is fire burning the liver

poem0093_qa.text_ar: سراجه وصالك والجفا ينفخ سراجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: My heart has ripened from distance, what need do you have to burn it? Your estrangement is embers and patience in your distance is the griddle

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وترى قصرنا اللي كم سنه نجمع ونبنيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Your love is imprinted in my heart and days cannot erase it. Your abandonment tries to stir it but cannot move it

poem0093_qa.text_ar: هوى العذل طيح بابه العود وسياجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Hope dies but thoughts of love revive it. The veins of loyalty pulse in soul and heart

poem0093_qa.text_ar: لي أيام مدري وين دارك ولا أدري ليه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Have you heard of someone crying for one who makes them cry? Have you heard of a prisoner who doesn't want freedom?

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وانا خابر اني باب شفك ومزلاجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: I love them and don't hate them for what they do. The necks of hope still bend toward them

poem0093_qa.text_ar: غياب بلا سبة، هجاد بلا تنبيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: May a dwelling, may a dwelling that embraces them be watered by seasonal rains. May the world smile at them and their brows smile back

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وجفا أغلى العرب نار على الكبد وهّاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: My mood has lost what entertains it from distress. My soul has become argumentative with people and night

poem0093_qa.text_ar: نضج قلبي من البعد
poem0093_qa.translation_en: I go to a place I know, know they visit. I direct my feet there though they have no need

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وش حاجتك تكويه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: What need do you have to burn/torment him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: جفاك الجمر والصبر في بعدك الصاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: The ember of your abandonment and patience burns in your distant coldness

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وطبع حبك بقلبي والأيام
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And the nature of your love is in my heart and the days

poem0093_qa.text_ar: ما تمحيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: It cannot be erased

poem0093_qa.text_ar: هذا هجرك يحاول يموجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: This is your abandonment trying to drown him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: ولا ماجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And nothing came to him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: يموت الأمل
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Hope dies

poem0093_qa.text_ar: لكن ظنون الغلا تحييه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: But the suspicions of love revive him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وعروق الوفاء في الروح والقلب وجاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And the veins of loyalty in the soul and heart are throbbing

poem0093_qa.text_ar: خبرتوا حد يبكي على واحد يبكيه؟
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Have you heard of someone crying over one who cries for them?

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وخبرتوا حد مسجون ما يبغي إفراجه؟
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Have you ever heard of a prisoner who doesn't want his release?

poem0093_qa.text_ar: أحبه ولا أبغضته على شيء يسويه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: I love him and never hate him for anything he does

poem0093_qa.text_ar: ولا زالت رقاب الرجا فيه منعاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And still the necks of hope remain bent towards him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: عسى منزل
poem0093_qa.translation_en: May there be a dwelling/home

poem0093_qa.text_ar: عسى منزل ضمّه حقوق الوسم تسقيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: May the dwelling that embraced the rights of the rainy season water it

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وتضحك له الدنيا وهو يضحك حجاجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: The world smiles at him while he laughs at its pilgrims

poem0093_qa.text_ar: فقد خاطري شيء من الضيقة مسلّيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Something has relieved my troubled heart from its distress

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وغدت روحي من الناس والليل هجاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And my soul became restless from people and the night

poem0093_qa.text_ar: أروح لمكان خابره
poem0093_qa.translation_en: I go to a place I know well

poem0093_qa.text_ar: خابره يأتيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: His news reaches him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: أسوق القدم صوبه وهي ما لها حاجة
poem0093_qa.translation_en: I drive my feet towards him though they have no need

poem0093_qa.text_ar: مكان نزيهٍ وأول مواجهي له فيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: A pristine place where my first encounter with him was there

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وأنا خابرِ ما رجل مثله بدوّاجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And I know there's no man like him in his generosity
poem0093_qa.imagery_tags: noble character, unique person, generous host

poem0093_qa.text_ar: لعل وعسى ما حدني للمجيء يدعيه وأشوفه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: Perhaps and maybe what urged me to come, he claims it and I see him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وأنا ما ودي أسبب إحراجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And I don't want to cause him embarrassment

poem0093_qa.text_ar: يحب الجريح آخر دروب اللقاء ويجيه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: The wounded one loves the last paths of meeting and it comes to him

poem0093_qa.text_ar: وتحب القدم جية مكان أول مواجه
poem0093_qa.translation_en: And the foot loves to return to the place of the first encounter

poem0094_qa.translation_en: Last night, God decreed for me and I encountered

poem0094_qa.text_ar: ـحكت ما ابغى الناس تدري يبلفيت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: I don't want people to know about my encounter
But against my will, tears flowed from my eyes
My tears were confused in my eyes and I turned away
I blinked wanting them not to show, but they fell

poem0094_qa.text_ar: عقبه برّق في العين برّاق واسقيت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: After that, lightning flashed in my eyes and I was watered
My cheeks and chest collided together

poem0094_qa.text_ar: وانا احسب اني في فراقه تعافيت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: And I thought I had recovered from his separation
And that my tears had moved away from the entrance of sustenance

poem0094_qa.text_ar: اللي يحسب إني على البعد سجّيت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: Those who think I've adapted to the distance
Or that my eyes have surrendered to sleep

poem0094_qa.text_ar: ياليتني من قبل لا أحبه أقفيت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: I wish I had turned back before I loved him

poem0094_qa.text_ar: ولا أن دروبي عن دروبه تناحت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: Or that my paths had diverged from his paths
It would be easier for me than crying and lamenting

poem0094_qa.text_ar: واشلى من سدودٍ على الناس باحت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: And better than barriers that revealed themselves to people

poem0094_qa.text_ar: عرفت كيف الحي يفرق عن الميت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: I learned how the living differs from the dead
And I knew the value of my blessing when it departed

poem0094_qa.text_ar: قولوا له أني عقب بعده تدانيت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: Tell him that after his departure I deteriorated
And my feet turned away from the paths of the healed

poem0094_qa.text_ar: شاحت، البعد نار وطاعة العذل كبريت
poem0094_qa.translation_en: Distance is fire and obeying blame is sulfur
His distance falls short
Indeed, the liver has exhaled its pain

poem0092_qa.text_ar: ووالله ولو تعتذر ما انت بعلى خبري
poem0092_qa.translation_en: By God, even if you apologize, you don't know my state

poem0092_qa.text_ar: والله لو ان حمل صبري فوقك ان تبرك
poem0092_qa.translation_en: By God, if the burden of my patience were upon you, you would kneel

poem0092_qa.text_ar: ووالله ولو تعتذر ما انت بعلى خبري
poem0092_qa.translation_en: By God, even if you apologize, you don't know my state

poem0092_qa.text_ar: ان ما جبرك الغلا مقدر على جبرك
poem0092_qa.translation_en: If love didn't mend you, I'm capable of mending you

poem0092_qa.text_ar: يا اللي على ارض الوفاء ما شبرك بشبري
poem0092_qa.translation_en: O you whose measure on the land of loyalty doesn't match mine
You get angry and return, finding me aware of you

poem0092_qa.text_ar: وانا انكسر لك وأجيك ادور لجبري
poem0092_qa.translation_en: And I break for you and come to you seeking my healing
I erase, become gentle and loving

poem0092_qa.text_ar: ولا أقول أبرك حسبي عليك أعشقك
poem0092_qa.translation_en: And I don't say leave me, God suffices me, I love you

poem0092_qa.text_ar: تزعل وترجع وتلقاني على خبرك وانا انكسر لك وأجيك
poem0092_qa.translation_en: You get angry and return, finding me aware of you, and I break for you and come
Seeking my healing, I erase, become gentle and loving

poem0092_qa.text_ar: ولا أقول أبرك حسبي عليك أعشقك
poem0092_qa.translation_en: And I don't say leave me, God suffices me, I love you

poem0091_qa.text_ar: الموت جاك وهو تقلبه الظنون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Death came to you while doubts were turning it over

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ولا لك الا الصبر ويش وداك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: You have nothing but patience, and what led you to it

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ان قلت ضايق قال هونها تهون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: If you say you're distressed, he says take it easy, it will ease

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ما يدري ان ضيقتك له وبكاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: He doesn't know that your distress is for him and your tears are for him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: تقعد تحبه وانت ساكت ومغبون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: You sit loving him while you're silent and wronged

poem0091_qa.text_ar: يمكن بيوم يقال له ما أوفاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Perhaps one day he'll be told how unfaithful you were to him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وكانك مشكك هو يمون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: As if you're doubting whether he'll grant favor

poem0091_qa.text_ar: او ما يمون تخيل ان عفت الصبر فرقاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Or he won't grant favor, imagine if patience wore out and separated you from him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: غلاه مو فوق الغلا ماهو بدون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: His value is not above precious, it's not without

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وحكاه سمعك يجبرك برخاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And his story, your hearing compels you to submit to him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ودك تحطه في العيون من العيون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: You wish to place him in the eyes from the eyes

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وتبسط على رمضاء الثرا يمناك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And spread your right hand on the burning earth for him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وتظله من الشمس في ظل الجفون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And shade him from the sun in the shadow of eyelids

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ولا يمر إذنه سوى لباك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And nothing passes his ear except your weeping for him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وتسج من غيره لو أنهم يذكرون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And you block others even if they mention

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ومنين ما سجت القدم ممشاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And wherever your foot blocks, your path is for him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: فلوٍ يميله الترف ميل الغصون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: A youth whom luxury inclines like the inclination of branches

poem0091_qa.text_ar: غصن ليان ومكرمه بسقاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: A flexible branch, honored by your watering of it

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وجديله إلا أقفى ستر منه المتون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And his braid, when it turns back, covers the backs from it

poem0091_qa.text_ar: كني بريحة لا التفت لدعاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: As if with a scent, he doesn't turn to your call to him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: يزهى الملابس أي لبس وأي لون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Clothes glorify him, any wear and any color

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وفي الليل وجهه لا ظهر قداك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And at night his face, no back appeared like him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: كيك وعسل لكن ما هو في صحون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Cake and honey but not in plates

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ودعاك من عين الحسود غطاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And your prayer from the envious eye covered him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: الله لا يعطيه ناسا ينظلون ويستر عليه من النحل لا يأكله
poem0091_qa.translation_en: May God not grant him people who oppress, and may He protect him from slander that doesn't consume him
poem0091_qa.imagery_tags: oppression, divine protection, slander as consumption

poem0091_qa.text_ar: كيك وعسل لكن ما هو في صحون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Cake and honey but not on plates

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ودعاك من عين الحسود غطاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And may the prayer protect you from the envious eye that covers you

poem0091_qa.text_ar: الله لا يعطيه ناسا ينظلون ويستر عليه من النحل لا يأكله
poem0091_qa.translation_en: May God not grant him people who oppress, and may He protect him from slander that consumes

poem0091_qa.text_ar: الوصف والتعبير في وصفه يخون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Description and expression betray when describing him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: غير اطلب الله يستجيب دعاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: I only ask that God answers your prayers for Him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وعذال قلبك في محبته يهبون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And the critics of your heart rush into his love

poem0091_qa.text_ar: سكينهم عن قطع حبل حاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Their knife cut the rope that was woven for him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: اللي من لحوم الأوادم ياكلون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Those who eat from the flesh of humans

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وحلوقهم دفع البلا متواكلة
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And their throats push away calamity, relying on one another

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ناس عن أعراض العرب ما يعرضون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: People who do not turn away from the honor of Arabs

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ودايم على نفس الشكل والشاكلة
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And always in the same form and manner

poem0091_qa.text_ar: في سلمهم أولى بالأكل الأقربون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: In their peace, the closest ones are most deserving of sustenance

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وأطيبهم اللي صيدته شكواك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And the best of them is the one to whom your complaint is his prey

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وعذال قلبك في محبته يهبون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And the critics of your heart rush into his love

poem0091_qa.text_ar: سكينهم عن قطع حبل حاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Their knife cut the rope that was woven for him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: اللي من لحوم الاوادم يأكلون وحلوقهم دفع البلاء
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Those who eat from the flesh of humans, may their throats be afflicted with calamity

poem0091_qa.text_ar: متواكلة ناس عن أعراض العرب ما يعرضون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: There are people who rely on others, they do not defend the honor of Arabs

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ودايم على نفس الشكل والشاكلة
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And always in the same form and manner

poem0091_qa.text_ar: في سلمهم أولى بالأكل الأقربون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: In their peace, the closest ones are most deserving of sustenance

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وأطيبهم اللي صيدته شكواك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And the best of them is the one to whom your complaint is his prey

poem0091_qa.text_ar: يدعيهم الشيطان للذنب ويجون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: The devil calls them to sin and they come

poem0091_qa.text_ar: هذا يقطع له وذاك يواكله
poem0091_qa.translation_en: This one cuts for him and that one feeds him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: يدعيهم الشيطان للذنب ويجون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Satan calls them to sin and they come

poem0091_qa.text_ar: هذا يقطع له وذاك يواكله
poem0091_qa.translation_en: This one cuts for him and that one feeds him

poem0091_qa.text_ar: يا جعلهم فدوة ثرا الغض الفتون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: May they be a ransom for the soil of the tender and captivating one

poem0091_qa.text_ar: اللي على الدرب الطويل خطاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: The one whose steps are on the long path

poem0091_qa.text_ar: طيفه إلا نام البشر زارك بهون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: His specter visits you gently only when people are asleep

poem0091_qa.text_ar: وطيفك إلا نام البشر سراك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And your specter, when people sleep, travels to him
poem0091_qa.imagery_tags: night visitation, dream, phantom, sleeping world

poem0091_qa.text_ar: حبه دفان ومقبرة خضر الطعون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: His love is a burial ground and a grave for green wounds

poem0091_qa.text_ar: بين العظام العوج كنك ناكله
poem0091_qa.translation_en: Among the crooked bones, as if you are eating it

poem0091_qa.text_ar: غروِ يميله الترف ميل الغصون
poem0091_qa.translation_en: A tender one whom luxury inclines like the swaying of branches

poem0091_qa.text_ar: ولا يوصف له غلاه حكاك له
poem0091_qa.translation_en: And no one can describe his worth when they speak of him

poem0064_om.text_ar: أنا أكثر انسان التقي فيه ابليس، وابليس ماهو انسان لكن بأنّسه، هالوقت كله بالقلب، واسأل الكيس العدل ولا الظلم يا اللي مكيّس؟ نحضر عزاء ودموع خلفه معاريس هذا دفن والده وهذا يعرّس دنيا وأقدار وصراحة
poem0064_om.translation_en: Subscribe to the channel

poem0064_om.text_ar: وأباليس وغيبة ونميمة وكذب كل حد يمارس
poem0064_om.translation_en: Devils, backbiting, gossip and lies - everyone practices

poem0064_om.text_ar: يلعب معانا الغرب شطرنج وبقيس كنس ملكنا والشعب صار يكنس
poem0064_om.translation_en: The West plays chess with us, sweeping our king while the people sweep

poem0064_om.text_ar: يلعب معانا الغرب شطرنج وبقيس، كنس ملكنا والشعب صار يكنس، علم العروبة لو وقف فيه تنكيس
poem0064_om.translation_en: The flag of Arabism, even if it stands at half-mast

poem0064_om.text_ar: ما دام حال الواقع اعوام منكس
poem0064_om.translation_en: As long as the state of reality remains years of setback
And the poetry of Arabism, even if smoothed and polished

poem0064_om.text_ar: شفت العرب من مص دمها تملس
poem0064_om.translation_en: I saw the Arabs become smooth from sucking their own blood
But our Arabs have gone

poem0064_om.text_ar: وأهل المكابيس حتى صحنا الفاضي قامت تلحس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And the people of hardships even licked the empty plate clean
But our Arabs have gone

poem0064_om.text_ar: وأهل المكابيس حتى صحنا الفاضي قامت تلحس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And the people of hardships even licked the empty plate
Jerusalem, its walls are the features of the oppressed
He wishes to feel that people hold him sacred

poem0064_om.text_ar: والموت في دمشق لازال في ريس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And death in Damascus is still at its peak

poem0064_om.text_ar: والطفل في بطن امه الموت رفّس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And the child in his mother's womb, death kicked
And we repeat without action or principles
Our subject is years that no one has felt
We repeat without action or principles
Our matter has been years with no one feeling it
Generally, sometimes we chat on Facebook
And we forget the worries and every injustice we touch

poem0064_om.text_ar: وفي الفيس أنثى في حلاها نسانيس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And on Facebook there's a woman whose beauty makes one forget

poem0064_om.text_ar: ياليت أنا الي بس دايم تفيّس
poem0064_om.translation_en: I wish I was the only one who always breathes/exists
And on Facebook
Female
In her beauty there are gentle breezes

poem0064_om.text_ar: ياليت أنا الي بس دايم تفيّس
poem0064_om.translation_en: I wish I was the only one who always breathes/exists
You take Yara's waist
And the features of Lamees
And every face
Her face has no equal, it bankrupts comparison
The one you reject, what is his anger with speculation
And whoever speaks to her thinks she's flirting
When she passes by you, she takes you to Paris

poem0064_om.text_ar: وكل عطر في باريس تقعد ترمس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And every perfume in Paris, you sit and whisper/murmur
When she passes by you, she takes you to Paris

poem0064_om.text_ar: وكل عطر في باريس تقعد ترمس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And every perfume in Paris, you sit and whisper/murmur

poem0064_om.text_ar: إن نامت، ينام القمر في كوابيس
poem0064_om.translation_en: When she sleeps, the moon sleeps in nightmares
And if you wish to visit the morning and breathe
But distance took her away
And distance is the foundation of wounds
Oh you who makes every relationship a competition

poem0064_om.text_ar: وان قفت أنفاس في عيوني متاعيس
poem0064_om.translation_en: And if breaths stopped in my eyes, weary and exhausted
And the face is cold, I wish you would come to warm it with sunshine
And I believe that sorrow produces feelings
Poetry is a mosque and mediocrity defiles it

poem0064_om.text_ar: ومن قفت ومن راحت وأنا مخاويه ابليس
poem0064_om.translation_en: Some stopped and some left while I remain accompanied by devils

poem0064_om.text_ar: يا رب تغفر لي إذا كنت مونس
poem0064_om.translation_en: O Lord, forgive me if I have been a companion

poem0062_om.text_ar: من ذُرى هيثم التاريخ شاهد عيان
poem0062_om.translation_en: From the peaks of Haitham, history is an eyewitness

poem0062_om.text_ar: من ذُرى هيثم التاريخ شاهد عيان
poem0062_om.translation_en: From the peaks of Haitham, history is an eyewitness

poem0062_om.text_ar: في ذُرى محمد التاريخ شامخ تليد
poem0062_om.translation_en: In the peaks of Mohammed, history stands tall and ancient

poem0062_om.text_ar: يا عسى عائلة يونس في روض الجنان
poem0062_om.translation_en: May the family of Younis be in the gardens of paradise

poem0062_om.text_ar: قلب يوم حزين في روح يوم مجيد
poem0062_om.translation_en: A sad day's heart in the soul of a glorious day

poem0062_om.text_ar: ضمنا وجه هيثم قبل شرح البيان
poem0062_om.translation_en: Haitham's face embraced us before the explanation

poem0062_om.text_ar: وكل بيت في عمان تحس فيه الفقيد
poem0062_om.translation_en: And every house in Oman feels the loss

poem0062_om.text_ar: دمت مولاي دام العز
poem0062_om.translation_en: Long live my lord, may glory last, may peace endure

poem0062_om.text_ar: دام الأمان
poem0062_om.translation_en: Consolation does not last in the state of Al Bu Said

poem0062_om.text_ar: ما يدوم العزاء في دولة البوسعيد
poem0062_om.translation_en: Stop, there are still feet to stand and be inspired

poem0062_om.text_ar: السلام عليكم ورحمة الله وبركاته
poem0062_om.translation_en: He departed but remains, even if dark clouds asked his lights to disappear

poem0062_om.text_ar: أمس بالخير على أساتذة أعضاء لجنة التحكيم الموقرة
poem0062_om.translation_en: On a night that rains departure and pens flow

poem0062_om.text_ar: أعضاء لجنة الاستشارية
poem0062_om.translation_en: For the loss of one whose presence was a dream's face, hard to repeat

poem0062_om.text_ar: الحضور الكريم والمشاهدين من أمام الشاشات
poem0062_om.translation_en: The sunset of voice from the pulse of places extinguishes the days

poem0062_om.text_ar: وأرحب بالقايم بأعمال السفارة في دولة الإمارات العربية المتحدة
poem0062_om.translation_en: The sunrise of longing in the Emirates' chest, its fire burns on every path

poem0062_om.text_ar: على حضور هذا اليوم
poem0062_om.translation_en: His steps embraced wedding and orphans' mourning

poem0062_om.text_ar: ولا أنسى
poem0062_om.translation_en: To the sick he comes from travel to visit

poem0062_om.text_ar: ولا أنسى أني أشكر الجمهور اللي تعنى لي وإخواني الزملاء في هذه الليلة
poem0062_om.translation_en: The hands of wind carried him in the procession of flags

poem0062_om.text_ar: وأشكر الجمهور الغفير اللي تعنى لي من سلطنة عمان
poem0062_om.translation_en: And people's hands sometimes embrace him and sometimes protect him

poem0062_om.text_ar: رغم الازدحام المروري والاحتفالات الوطنية
poem0062_om.translation_en: As if Al Ain wasn't a country, it was from the wombs

poem0062_om.text_ar: اسأل الله التوفيق في القصيدة
poem0062_om.translation_en: How much he lived as Al Ain's eye, dedicating his life to his home

poem0062_om.text_ar: قِفا لا زال باقي للوقوف أقدام واستلهام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Because the palms of Hili spread about him tales of generosity

poem0062_om.text_ar: قِفا لا زال باقي للوقوف أقدام واستلهام
poem0062_om.translation_en: A date bunch from Mazyad hangs over Buraimi as good tidings

poem0062_om.text_ar: رحل باقي لو الغيم العتيم استأذن أنواره تغيب
poem0062_om.translation_en: He departed, yet remains, even if the dark clouds asked permission for its lights to disappear

poem0062_om.text_ar: في ليلة تمطر ذهاب وسالت الأقلام
poem0062_om.translation_en: On a night that rains departure and the pens flowed

poem0062_om.text_ar: لفقد الي وجوده وجه حلم وصعب تكراره
poem0062_om.translation_en: For the loss of one whose presence was a dream's face, difficult to repeat

poem0062_om.text_ar: غروب الصوت عن نبض الأماكن يطفي الأيام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The voice's sunset from the pulse of places extinguishes the days

poem0062_om.text_ar: شروق الشوق في صدر الإمارة يشتعل ناره على كل درب
poem0062_om.translation_en: The sunrise of longing in the chest of the emirate ignites its fire on every path

poem0062_om.text_ar: عانق خطوته عرس وعزاء أيتام
poem0062_om.translation_en: His step embraces both wedding and orphans' mourning

poem0062_om.text_ar: على مرضى يجيهم من سفر لزيارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: Upon the sick who are visited by those coming from travel

poem0062_om.text_ar: زوارة أيادي الريح كانت تحمله في موكب الأعلام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The visiting hands of the wind were carrying him in the procession of flags

poem0062_om.text_ar: وأيادي الناس تارة تحضنه وتحصّنه تارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: And people's hands sometimes embrace him and sometimes fortify him

poem0062_om.text_ar: أيادي الريح كانت تحمله في موكب الأعلام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The hands of the wind were carrying him in the procession of flags

poem0062_om.text_ar: وأيادي الناس تارة تحضنه وتحصّنه تارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: And people's hands sometimes embrace him and sometimes fortify him

poem0062_om.text_ar: كأن العين ما كانت بلد كانت من الأرحام
poem0062_om.translation_en: As if the eye was not a country, it was from the wombs

poem0062_om.text_ar: كثر ما عاش عين العين عمّر عمره لداره
poem0062_om.translation_en: As long as the apple of the eye lived, he spent his life for his home

poem0062_om.text_ar: لأن نخيل هيلي ناشرة عنه سير إكرام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Because the palm trees of my beloved spread tales of generosity about him

poem0062_om.text_ar: تدلى عذق من مزيد على البريمي بشارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: A date cluster descended from Mazeed upon Al Buraimi as good tidings

poem0062_om.text_ar: كأن العين ما كانت بلد
poem0062_om.translation_en: As if the eye was never a homeland

poem0062_om.text_ar: كانت من الأرحام
poem0062_om.translation_en: She was from the wombs/kinship

poem0062_om.text_ar: كثر ما عاش عين العين عمر عمره لداره
poem0062_om.translation_en: As long as the apple of the eye lived his lifetime in his home

poem0062_om.text_ar: لأن نخيل هيلي ناشرة عنه سير الإكرام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Because the palm trees of my beloved spread tales of generosity about him

poem0062_om.text_ar: تدلى عذق من مزيد على البريمي
poem0062_om.translation_en: A date cluster hangs from Mazeed over Al Buraimi
poem0062_om.imagery_tags: date palm, fruit, place names, hanging cluster

poem0062_om.text_ar: بشارة رسم لمبزّرة
poem0062_om.translation_en: A herald's sketch for a harbinger

poem0062_om.text_ar: وتشكلت من طيبه الأنسام
poem0062_om.translation_en: And the breezes were formed from his kindness

poem0062_om.text_ar: ومزرعة الشويب تلونت من واحة أفكاره
poem0062_om.translation_en: And the Shuwaib farm was colored by the oasis of his thoughts

poem0062_om.text_ar: ماعليكم زود
poem0062_om.translation_en: You have no burden/It's not too much for you

poem0062_om.text_ar: تسامى في رثاء طحنون بن محمد الإلهام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Inspiration rose high in the elegy for Tahnoun bin Mohammed

poem0062_om.text_ar: حبر تاريخ من روح الأثر يتنفس أخباره
poem0062_om.translation_en: Ink of history from the soul of heritage breathes its stories
poem0062_om.imagery_tags: ink, history, soul, heritage, breathing, stories

poem0062_om.text_ar: تسامى في رثاء طحنون بن محمد الإلهام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Inspiration rose high in the elegy for Tahnoun bin Mohammed

poem0062_om.text_ar: حبر تاريخ من روح الأثر يتنفس أخباره
poem0062_om.translation_en: Ink of history from the soul of heritage breathes its stories
poem0062_om.imagery_tags: ink, history, soul, heritage, breathing, stories

poem0062_om.text_ar: نعاه الشعر والموروث وزمول لها أرقام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Poetry and heritage mourned him, and camels bearing numbers for it

poem0062_om.text_ar: وصقر من السماء عوّد يدور عن سمي سارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: And a falcon from the sky trained to circle searching for one named Sarah

poem0062_om.text_ar: نعاه الشعر والموروث وزمول لها أرقام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Poetry and heritage mourned him, and camels bearing numbers for it

poem0062_om.text_ar: وصقر من السماء عوّد يدور عن سمي سارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: And a falcon from the sky trained to circle searching for one named Sarah

poem0062_om.text_ar: حضارة صاغت أطباعه
poem0062_om.translation_en: A civilization that shaped his character

poem0062_om.text_ar: وأصالة عتّقت هندام
poem0062_om.translation_en: And authenticity has aged/refined the attire

poem0062_om.text_ar: هوية أرض حتى بين ثاني
poem0062_om.translation_en: The identity of a land even between seconds

poem0062_om.text_ar: وأول زرارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: And the first button

poem0062_om.text_ar: سند زايد
poem0062_om.translation_en: The support of Zayed

poem0062_om.text_ar: سند زايد قبل تتعطر الصحراء نفل وخزام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The support of Zayed, before the desert becomes fragrant with clover and lavender

poem0062_om.text_ar: عصامه
poem0062_om.translation_en: Self-made

poem0062_om.text_ar: يوم وحدها سبع جنات مدرارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: One day alone, seven abundant paradises

poem0062_om.text_ar: حزامه
poem0062_om.translation_en: Her belt

poem0062_om.text_ar: يوم عمرها خليفة وارتقت أحلام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The day Khalifa became her age and dreams ascended

poem0062_om.text_ar: حسامه
poem0062_om.translation_en: His sword

poem0062_om.text_ar: لما صدرها محمد فوق مسباره
poem0062_om.translation_en: When Muhammad placed her chest above the probe/measure

poem0062_om.text_ar: سند زايد
poem0062_om.translation_en: The support of Zayed

poem0062_om.text_ar: سند زايد قبل تتعطر الصحراء نفل وخزام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The support of Zayed, before the desert becomes fragrant with clover and lavender

poem0062_om.text_ar: عصامه
poem0062_om.translation_en: Self-made

poem0062_om.text_ar: يوم وحدها سبع جنات مدرارة
poem0062_om.translation_en: One day alone, seven abundant paradises

poem0062_om.text_ar: حزامه
poem0062_om.translation_en: Her belt

poem0062_om.text_ar: يوم عمرها خليفة وارتقت أحلام
poem0062_om.translation_en: The day Khalifa became her age and dreams ascended

poem0062_om.text_ar: حسامه
poem0062_om.translation_en: His sword

poem0062_om.text_ar: لما صدرها محمد فوق مسباره
poem0062_om.translation_en: When Muhammad placed her chest above the probe/measure

poem0062_om.text_ar: مسيرة بر
poem0062_om.translation_en: A journey of righteousness

poem0062_om.text_ar: لفّت في ثمانين العمر إحرام
poem0062_om.translation_en: He wrapped himself in the ihram at eighty years of age
poem0062_om.imagery_tags: elderly pilgrim, ihram garment, hajj, old age

poem0062_om.text_ar: من الإخلاص
poem0062_om.translation_en: From sincerity/devotion

poem0062_om.text_ar: وفى رحلة العامين بإصراره
poem0062_om.translation_en: And in the two-year journey with determination

poem0062_om.text_ar: قفا نفخر
poem0062_om.translation_en: Stand, let us be proud

poem0062_om.text_ar: قفا نفخر ولا نبكي
poem0062_om.translation_en: We stand to be proud and not to weep

poem0062_om.text_ar: سحب هزاعها قدام
poem0062_om.translation_en: Clouds whose thunder is ahead

poem0062_om.text_ar: ومطر باقي في ذاكرة المدينة عشرة أنهارا
poem0062_om.translation_en: And rain remains in the city's memory as ten rivers

poem0062_om.text_ar: شكرا لكم
poem0062_om.translation_en: Thank you

poem0062_om.text_ar: سلامتكم
poem0062_om.translation_en: Your safety/wellbeing

poem0062_om.text_ar: ماعليكم زود
poem0062_om.translation_en: You don't need to worry too much

poem0062_om.text_ar: ماعليكم زود الله يبيض وجيهكم
poem0062_om.translation_en: May God brighten your faces

poem0062_om.text_ar: الله يبيض وجيهكم
poem0062_om.translation_en: May God brighten your faces

poem0040_sa.text_ar: بالنسبة له يقول التوجد يقول
poem0040_sa.translation_en: In relation to him, he says there is none, he says

poem0040_sa.text_ar: كل ما فاضت عيوني نجر بن حربان دنّا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Whenever my eyes overflow, the son of Harban approaches

poem0040_sa.text_ar: في ضلوعي يسمع اللي من وراء بغداد دنّة
poem0040_sa.translation_en: In my ribs, those beyond Baghdad hear the echo

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يوم ابن مدعث لنا سنّ الوجود الحق
poem0040_sa.translation_en: When Ibn Mad'ath established for us the law of true existence

poem0040_sa.text_ar: سنّة وآفوادي كن دود الأرض يرعى في مسنّى
poem0040_sa.translation_en: A law, and my heart like earth worms grazing in its pasture

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وآفوادي لو تظنّه يا ابن مدعث ما تظنه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And my heart, if you think it, O Ibn Mad'ath, you don't think it

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وآفوادي لو تظنّه يا ابن مدعث ما تظنه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And my heart, if you think it, O Ibn Mad'ath, you don't think it

poem0040_sa.text_ar: كنّه المجدور عقب الجور مرميٍ
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Like one with smallpox after injustice, thrown down

poem0040_sa.text_ar: بعنّة أشعل التنباك من خلجته ما شبّه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Stubbornly lighting tobacco from his throat, not satisfied

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وكنّه مير كنّه من عجاج فاح من صدره
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Like bitter, like dust rising from his chest

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يكنه يا وجودي وجد من هو عظ بابهامه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Like my existence, the passion of one who bit his thumb

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وحنّى واحد شاف الثلاثة البيض سودٍ
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And one who saw the three whites turned black

poem0040_sa.text_ar: مستجن شاف من حول الفريج خشيف ريمٍ
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Crazed, saw around the neighborhood a gazelle's rustle

poem0040_sa.text_ar: مرجهنه وثوّر البندق بصيده وراع الخشف
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Confused, fired his gun at his prey and startled the fawn

poem0040_sa.text_ar: عنّا طلق أربع عقبه أربع
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Stubbornly fired four, then four more

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ثم سمع صوتٍ يونّا وأثرها أمه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Then heard a voice moaning, and it was his mother

poem0040_sa.text_ar: أصابها طلقة وطار العقل منه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: A bullet hit her and his mind flew away

poem0040_sa.text_ar: واتهموه بذبحة أمه ليلة العيد
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And they accused him of killing his mother on Eid night

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وتجنى أتهمته الناس وصدوف المقادير
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And falsely people accused him and fate turned away

poem0040_sa.text_ar: أتهمنه قالوا أخوانه ترى ما انا بمنك ولا انت
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They accused him, his brothers said you're not from us nor we

poem0040_sa.text_ar: منا يفرق الله بيننا وبينك عسى مالك
poem0040_sa.translation_en: May God not separate between us and you, hopefully you have nothing

poem0040_sa.text_ar: مضنة لك ثلاثة ايام من الحين وارحل من وطننا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: You have three days from now to leave our homeland

poem0040_sa.text_ar: عقبها تخطر على راسك مضاريب
poem0040_sa.translation_en: After that, troubles come to your mind

poem0040_sa.text_ar: الأسنة ودّع مريته وتو بنته صغيرة
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Last year he bid farewell to his wife while his daughter was still young

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ما تحنى توها ام اربع شهور
poem0040_sa.translation_en: She hasn't given birth yet, she's four months pregnant

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وفي لحمها زود لنة رسب غوجه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And in her flesh there is extra softness that captivates the heart

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وانقلب جبله بين الأمات وان والحمايا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And his mountain turned among the mothers while the protectors

poem0040_sa.text_ar: من خلافه ارخوا حبال الأعنة المطايا والسبايا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: From his departure they loosened the reins of the mounts and the captives

poem0040_sa.text_ar: من خلافه عمدنه والمنايا سجّد قدام عينه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: From his opposition we intended him, and death prostrated before his eyes

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يرجبنه كل ما عنز على قوم رطلنا الراس طنا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They respond whenever we pass by people, we nod our heads heavily
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: head nodding, passing by, social interaction

poem0040_sa.text_ar: قالوا اذلف ذابح أمه لعنبونا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They said the one who approaches has slaughtered his mother for Anbouna

poem0040_sa.text_ar: لو نحن ذابح أمه لو زبنا عده أنه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: If we slaughtered his mother or if we wanted to count him

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ما زبنا لعنبوچ بيوت قوم ذابح أمه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We don't want your grapes, houses of people who killed their mother

poem0040_sa.text_ar: زبننه الجمالة ما تجمّل في ولد حر
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The beauty of a woman does not beautify herself for a free man's son

poem0040_sa.text_ar: تدنى مير دور غيرنا وانحش بقلبك
poem0040_sa.translation_en: You approach and pass by others' homes while shyness fills your heart

poem0040_sa.text_ar: لا ندنه وانقلب من عندهم كنه على محماس
poem0040_sa.translation_en: When we approached him, he turned away from them as if on hot coals
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: heat, burning coals, turning away, discomfort

poem0040_sa.text_ar: بن يمة اهل الغوص عجلات الركايب
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Near the pearl divers' people, the wheels of riding camels
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: pearl divers, camels, wheels, sea and desert

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وجهنه ركب ما وراء البحر ودموعينه يذرفنا فوق خده
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We directed our caravan beyond the sea while our tears flow down upon his cheek

poem0040_sa.text_ar: كنه أثواب بدو مرثعنه ويوم حل الليل
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Like the garments of Bedouins, tattered and worn, when night falls

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وأشرعة السفينة دودلنا والهبايب ما يخلن
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And the ship's sails swayed for us while the winds never cease

poem0040_sa.text_ar: محبل ما دودلنه قام ربان السفينة قال يا ناس امتحنا قوموا ادعوا ربكم
poem0040_sa.translation_en: When the ship's captain stood up and said O people we are being tested, rise and pray to your Lord
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: ship, captain, storm, prayer, divine test

poem0040_sa.text_ar: علام ما وسط الأجنة قال شيخ منهم الا عندي الرأي
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Why hasn't any sheikh among the embryos spoken except that I have the opinion

poem0040_sa.text_ar: المطنا ضحوا بواحد عسى الله ينقضي دينٍ
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The rain sacrificed one, may God fulfill a debt

poem0040_sa.text_ar: رجنه وطاحت البرأة على الي ذابح أمه ما تتنى ثم رموا به في ظلمات البحور
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They shackled him and the needle fell on the one who killed his mother, she doesn't bend, then they threw him into the darkness of the seas

poem0040_sa.text_ar: المستكنة وادبحوا عنه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The hidden one, and slaughter for him

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وادبحوا عنه وفي غبة هالموج يتثنى ثم عرض له من وحوش القرش جرجور
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And slaughter for him while in the midst of these waves he sways, then a shark beast appeared to him
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: waves, swaying motion, shark, beast, sea danger

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وصنا عض جرجور البحر ساقه وصايح واستجن أبك لله كلاه القرش ما يسلم
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Our friend bit the sea turtle's leg and screamed, went mad crying to God that the shark ate him and won't let go
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: sea turtle, shark attack, ocean predator, screaming, divine plea

poem0040_sa.text_ar: مطنا وفي ديار من بلاد الهند بين إنسٍ وجنّ من فضل ربي عليه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We traveled and in lands from India's regions, among humans and jinn, by my Lord's grace upon him
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: travel, India, humans, jinn, divine grace

poem0040_sa.text_ar: أطراف الأمواج حذفنه مثل ما قبل امس متهوم بذبح أمه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The edges of waves we trimmed like someone two days ago obsessed with slaughtering his mother

poem0040_sa.text_ar: معنّى أتهموه بذبح سلطان البلاد المطمئنة وفي السجون المظلمة خمسة عشر عاما
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They tortured one they accused of killing the sultan of the peaceful lands, and in dark prisons for fifteen years

poem0040_sa.text_ar: قضنا مع ثلاثة ما قضن الا عقب حاله
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We spent time with three who only settled after much difficulty

poem0040_sa.text_ar: قضنه لا سمع ورقىً تغني جر مسحوبه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We spent time when he heard a melody singing, drawn and prolonged

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وغنى وكل ما مضنون يزعج غصب تجي تاليه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And he sang, and everything precious disturbs, forcefully comes after it

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ونّة لا طرت لي بيوت قبل بالخلايا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: A moan when I flew to houses before in the cells/hives

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يرفعنه دار دولاب الدبا الدالوب دولابه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They raise it, the house of the wheel of fate, the wheel that turns its wheel

poem0040_sa.text_ar: و زنّه كل ما برقٍ شرا شرق لشروق الغيث
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And its weight whenever lightning flashes from east to the eastern rain

poem0040_sa.text_ar: شنّه كنه قلبه فوق سزب طيور برٍ شطّرنه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: As if his heart is above a flock of wild birds that have scattered

poem0040_sa.text_ar: انه عقب مدة جاه من داره
poem0040_sa.translation_en: He came from his home after a period of time

poem0040_sa.text_ar: نذير مرتعنه قال أنا جيتك بعلم
poem0040_sa.translation_en: A trembling warner said I came to you with knowledge

poem0040_sa.text_ar: والله انه حرمتك ماتت لها عشر سنوات
poem0040_sa.translation_en: By God, your wife has been dead for ten years

poem0040_sa.text_ar: قد مضن والبنية
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They have passed while the structure remains
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: time passing, buildings, permanence vs transience

poem0040_sa.text_ar: معمسٍ تقرع عليك إقراع
poem0040_sa.translation_en: A confusing matter that knocks upon you repeatedly

poem0040_sa.text_ar: شنة عمها عزر بها في وسط بيته
poem0040_sa.translation_en: What did her uncle do, he supported her in the middle of his house

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ما تهنى خادمة رسوانها الثنتين
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The servant does not find peace with her two masters

poem0040_sa.text_ar: واللي ضيفنه ثم زوجها لابو سبعين عام وما قصنّ شايبٍ يطرب خفوقه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And the one we hosted then married her off to a seventy-year-old, and didn't cut short an old man whose heart rejoices

poem0040_sa.text_ar: لا سمع للقرش رنة ينسم هروجه وان فاضت عيونه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: When he hears the coin's ring, his words flow sweetly, and his eyes overflow with tears
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: coin ringing, flowing words, overflowing eyes, tears

poem0040_sa.text_ar: و ازحرنه وسط سجنه كنه اللي ساكنٍ له في مجنّة
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And we crawled in the middle of our prison like one who dwells in his madness

poem0040_sa.text_ar: قال يا الربع افزعوني مسجنٍ غيظنه وقبر قلبي الدم دمغا يديكم
poem0040_sa.translation_en: He said O neighbors help me, I am imprisoned by his anger, and the grave of my heart bleeds in your hands

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يظهرنه قالوا وانا وش لنا والله انه ما شحننا غير بنتك
poem0040_sa.translation_en: They said she appeared, and I said what do we have to do with it, by God we only loaded your daughter

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ولا مثلك ما بقربه قرب جنة وانتحوا وعيون قايدهم جنوبه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: There is none like you, near whom is the closeness of paradise, and they turned away while their leader's eyes are towards the south

poem0040_sa.text_ar: خايل انه هو وراء سجنه عيونه كل نجم
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We thought he was behind his prison, his eyes are every star

poem0040_sa.text_ar: خايل انه عقب عام من مجيء ربعه وكن من عضّ
poem0040_sa.translation_en: He thought after a year of his people's arrival, as if from a bite

poem0040_sa.text_ar: سنه عفو الكفار عنه من عقب سود داهمنه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: A year of the infidels' pardon from him after dark calamities befell us

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يوم فكوا قيد رجله
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The day they untied the shackles from his leg

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يوم فكوا قيد رجله وانطلق خاطره منا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The day they freed his leg from chains and his heart was released from us

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وانطلق يبغى ديار من ورا نجد
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And he set off seeking lands beyond Najd
poem0040_sa.imagery_tags: travel, distant lands, Najd plateau, journey

poem0040_sa.text_ar: شحننه حيث ساقه ما يشوف إلا بنيته
poem0040_sa.translation_en: We loaded him up until his legs could only see his own structure

poem0040_sa.text_ar: يوم قنا من عقب عشرين عام وفي وطن قومٍ
poem0040_sa.translation_en: When we met after twenty years in the homeland of a people

poem0040_sa.text_ar: مصنة ومن ردى حظه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Protected yet from one whose fortune is poor

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ومن ردى حظه نهاره وان عيونه رقدن فز منه
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And whoever has bad luck, his day becomes troubled, and even when his eyes sleep, he startles from it

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ومن عقب حظه عضه حنشتاب
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And after his luck, a snake's fang bit him

poem0040_sa.text_ar: بسنه نغسل ملح شي واطبخنه احزي الواحد
poem0040_sa.translation_en: With a year we wash the salt of something and cook it, I tell the one

poem0040_sa.text_ar: سود الأفاعي طيحنه يوم وفا تذكر الا فوق النجوم يبرقنا
poem0040_sa.translation_en: The black serpents brought us down the day loyalty was remembered only above the stars shining upon us

poem0040_sa.text_ar: وشاف مشية بنته اللي جاية أبيها بينهن قال يانجوم السما تكفين روحي
poem0040_sa.translation_en: And he saw his daughter's walk as she came to her father, among them he said 'O stars of the sky, protect my soul'

poem0040_sa.text_ar: صوب أهلنا علمي بنتي. علمي بنتي على اللي صاب أبوها واستسنى
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Towards our people, teach my daughter. Teach my daughter about what befell her father and he waited patiently

poem0040_sa.text_ar: اشهد انه ما بيحلل هالليالي. اشهد انه مير حظي خانني
poem0040_sa.translation_en: I testify that these nights won't become lawful. I testify that my bitter luck has betrayed me

poem0040_sa.text_ar: ثم مات. ما به من يقنه ذاك وجده وجد حالي في وطننا من
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Then he died. There's no one to convince him that his longing is the longing of my state in our homeland from our thirst

poem0040_sa.text_ar: عطنه من هنوف خدرها بيض الغنادير
poem0040_sa.translation_en: Subscribe to the channel

poem0041_sa.text_ar:
لكن أقول 
ناقتي يا ناقتي
 لا رباع ولا سديس
ناقتي يا ناقتي
 لا رباع ولا سديس 
وصليني لا بيتي من ورا هاك الطعوس
 حايل رابع سنة من خيار العيس عيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: My she-camel, O my she-camel, neither four nor six years old, take me to my home beyond those sand dunes, a four-year-old from the finest white camels

poem0041_sa.text_ar: خفها لا درهمت كنه بالنار محسوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Her hooves when they strike are like fire being felt

poem0041_sa.text_ar: في دجى خالي خلا لا حسيس ولا نسيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: In empty darkness, neither sound nor human presence

poem0041_sa.text_ar: تدوخك من شدة البرد طقطقة الضروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The chattering of teeth from intense cold makes you dizzy

poem0041_sa.text_ar: في عيوني يكفخ الطير ويطيح الفريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: In my eyes, the bird is struck down and the prey falls

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وانتهض جساس من دون خالته البسوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And Jassas rises up for his aunt Al-Basous

poem0041_sa.text_ar: أقول في عيوني يكفخ الطير ويطيح الفريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: I say in my eyes, the bird is struck down and the prey falls

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وانتهض جساس من دون خالته البسوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And Jassas rises up for his aunt Al-Basous

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وفي خفوقي فرخ جنية يضرس ضريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And in my heart, a jinn's offspring gnashes fiercely

poem0041_sa.text_ar: يوم أورّدها هجوس وأصدرها هجوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: When I bring her with suspicion and send her away with suspicion

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ديرتي دار ابن ضاري وابن غثلم جريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: My homeland is the land of Ibn Dhari and Ibn Ghathlem Jareis

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ديرتي اللي دوجوا في فيافي نجد جوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: My homeland where they roamed the deserts of Najd as warriors

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ديرتي يا بنت شيخٍ ومعدنها نفيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: My homeland, O daughter of a sheikh with precious origins

poem0041_sa.text_ar: عيطموس دونها دثرتني عيطموس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Darkness before her, darkness covered me

poem0041_sa.text_ar: جادل مطرق جسدها مع الغربي يميس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Arguing, her body sways with the western wind

poem0041_sa.text_ar: طينةٍ من تربة الخلد والدم محموس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Clay from paradise's soil and blood inflamed

poem0041_sa.text_ar: نقشت خمسٍ من الخمس في يوم الخميس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: She inscribed five of the five on Thursday

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ليت شعري ما أرادت بهذا دختنوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Would that I knew what she meant by this riddle

poem0041_sa.text_ar: نقشت خمسٍ من الخمس في يوم الخميس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: She inscribed five of the five on Thursday

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ليت شعري ما أرادت بهذا دختنوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Would that I knew what she meant by this riddle

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كم تمنى ان معه مثلها فتنة إبليس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: How many wished to have with him like her, the temptation of Iblis

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كان يمكن قد قلب نصف أمتنا مجوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: He could have turned half our nation into Magians

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كن دماث الصدر منها كنيسة أو كنيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: As if the soft parts of her chest were a church or synagogue

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كن دماث الصدر منها كنيسة أو كنيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: As if the soft parts of her chest were a church or synagogue

poem0041_sa.text_ar: صومعة رهبان دير يؤدون الطقوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: A monastery of monks performing rituals

poem0041_sa.text_ar: أتقوس مع تقوس نواهدها وأقيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: I arch with the arching of her breasts and measure
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: body curves, feminine form, physical harmony

poem0041_sa.text_ar: قبتين قاب قوسين قوسٍ جنب قوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Two domes, two bow-lengths, a bow beside a bow
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: domes, bows, sacred geometry, spiritual distance

poem0041_sa.text_ar: أتقوس مع تقوس نواهدها وأقيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: I arch with the arching of her breasts and measure
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: body curves, feminine form, physical harmony

poem0041_sa.text_ar: قبتين قاب قوسين قوسٍ جنب قوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Two domes, two bow-lengths, a bow beside a bow
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: domes, bows, sacred geometry, spiritual distance

poem0041_sa.text_ar: خصرها يحكي لنا واقع القوم التعيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Her waist tells us the miserable reality of the people

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وردفها عن غطرسة بوش يعطينا دروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And her response about Bush's arrogance giving us lessons

poem0041_sa.text_ar: خصرها يحكي لنا واقع القوم التعيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Her waist tells us the miserable reality of the people

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وردفها عن غطرسة بوش يعطينا دروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And her response about Bush's arrogance giving us lessons

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وبالرغم من قصرها ما يجالسها جليس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And despite its shortness, no companion sits with it

poem0041_sa.text_ar: تصبح أطول نسوة الأرض في وضع الجلوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: She becomes the tallest of Earth's women when sitting down

poem0041_sa.text_ar: العروسة
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The bride

poem0041_sa.text_ar: العروسة ماتبي غير أبو تركي عريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The bride wants no one but Abu Turki as her groom

poem0041_sa.text_ar: العروسة نجد ما هيب حيّ الله عروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The bride is Najd, not just any bride, God bless her

poem0041_sa.text_ar: العروسة ماتبي غير أبو تركي عريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The bride wants no one but Abu Turki as her groom

poem0041_sa.text_ar: العروسة نجد ما هيب حيّ الله عروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The bride is Najd, not just any bride, God bless her

poem0041_sa.text_ar: نجد بنت المجد
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Najd is the daughter of glory

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ونسة مونسة الونيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The companion of the lonely one

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ما عسفها الا معزي
poem0041_sa.translation_en: None has wronged her except one who cherishes her

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ضحى اليوم العبوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The morning of the gloomy day

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كل أهل نجد هلي
poem0041_sa.translation_en: All the people of Najd are my family

poem0041_sa.text_ar: من أجا حتى الخميس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: From Monday until Thursday

poem0041_sa.text_ar: في ذراهم بالتجي
poem0041_sa.translation_en: In their protection I seek refuge

poem0041_sa.text_ar: عن مجففة الرسوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: About the one with dried/withered heads

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وعيشتي ما بين أسود
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And my life is among lions

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ولو ماني رئيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Even if I'm not a leader/president

poem0041_sa.text_ar: خير من كوني رئيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Better than being a president

poem0041_sa.text_ar: على شلقة تيوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: On a herd of goats

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وعيشتي ما بين أسود ولو ماني رئيس خير من كوني رئيس على شلقة
poem0041_sa.translation_en: My life among lions even if I'm not a leader is better than being a leader over hyenas

poem0041_sa.text_ar: تيوس. وليس عيبا إن خدعني بمكره كل هيس لان ما يفهم بطبع الهيوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Goats. And it's no shame if I'm deceived by the cunning of every fool, for one cannot understand the nature of goats

poem0041_sa.text_ar: الا الهيوس.
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Except for the madness/recklessness

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وليس عيبا إن خدعني بمكره كل هيس لان ما يفهم بطبع الهيوس الا الهيوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: It is no shame if I am deceived by the cunning of every scoundrel, for none understand the nature of scoundrels except scoundrels

poem0041_sa.text_ar: مصطفى في مأدبة عمه البابا لويس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Mustafa at the banquet of his uncle Pope Louis

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كبر اللقمة تحت ضوء حمراء الكؤوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The morsel grew large under the red light of the cups
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: food, wine cups, red light, feast

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وطسم حدتها على حائط المبكى جديس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And Tasm, its blade against the wall of weeping, Jadis
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: ancient tribes, wall of lamentation, blade, destruction

poem0041_sa.text_ar: يوم شقت ثوبها بنت عفار الشموس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The day the daughter of Affar tore her dress, the suns

poem0041_sa.text_ar: سيدة هاك القصر اسمها الاول لميس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The lady of this palace, her first name is Lamees

poem0041_sa.text_ar: دونها سمر الحناجر بترها حد موس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Without it, the brown throats are severed by the edge of a razor

poem0041_sa.text_ar: قسقست لأحرارهم قيس قيس قيس قيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: I divided for their free men portions upon portions upon portions

poem0041_sa.text_ar: قسقست لأحرارهم قيس قيس قيس قيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: I distributed to their free men, measure by measure, portion by portion

poem0041_sa.text_ar: والبستهم من مهانتهم مهانتهم انواع اللبوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And I dressed them in their humiliation with various types of garments of disgrace

poem0041_sa.text_ar: بين خدعة كعب أخيلٍ ومولد ادونيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Between the deception of Achilles' heel and the birth of Adonis

poem0041_sa.text_ar: درسونا الآلهة والمدرس هوماروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: They taught us about the gods, and the teacher was Homer

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ويش لاقوا من قناة السويس اهل السويس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: What did the people of Suez find from the Suez Canal
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: Suez Canal, local people, economic disparity

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وويش لاقوا من تلمسان قومٌ من سنوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And what did they find from Tlemcen, a people from Senussi

poem0041_sa.text_ar: كلهم من شدة الجوع راحوا خندريس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: They all went to Khandarees from the severity of hunger

poem0041_sa.text_ar: بين غيبوبة مخدر وصحوة عرق سوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Between the stupor of anesthesia and the awakening of licorice root
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: medical, herbal medicine, consciousness, traditional remedies

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ثورة راحوا سببها بتوع الأوتوبيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: A revolution they went, its cause was the bus people

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وش نبي فيها وهي ماتهز الا الغروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: What do we want from her when she only shakes the saplings

poem0041_sa.text_ar: آفة المرء اثنتان
poem0041_sa.translation_en: A person's afflictions are two

poem0041_sa.text_ar: الف كاس وحب كيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: A thousand cups and love of a pouch/bag

poem0041_sa.text_ar: والنفوس ليا بغيت تعرفها
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And the souls, when you want to know them

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ارم الفلوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Throw the money

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ينكشف زيف الشعارات
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The falsehood of slogans is exposed

poem0041_sa.text_ar: آفة المرء اثنتان
poem0041_sa.translation_en: A person's afflictions are two

poem0041_sa.text_ar: الف كاس وحب كيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: A thousand cups and love of a pouch/bag

poem0041_sa.text_ar: والنفوس ليا بغيت تعرفها
poem0041_sa.translation_en: And souls, when you want to know them

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ارم الفلوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Throw the money

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ينكشف زيف الشعارات
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The falsehood of slogans is exposed

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ليا حمي الوطيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: When the battle intensifies

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ترجع الاذناب اذناب وتبقى الروس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Tails return as tails and heads remain as heads

poem0041_sa.text_ar: روس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Heads/Leaders

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ينكشف زيف الشعارات إلى حمي الوطيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: The falsehood of slogans is exposed when the battle intensifies
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: battle, heat of conflict, masks falling, revelation

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ترجع الأذناب أذناب وتبقى الروس روس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Tails return as tails and heads remain as heads

poem0041_sa.text_ar: صدق لا قال العرب ما يحوص الا الخسيس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Truly as the Arabs say, only the vile person pries and meddles

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ولا يوطي الروس شيء مثل ضعف النفوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Nothing humbles heads like the weakness of souls

poem0041_sa.text_ar: ناقتي يا ناقتي لا رباع ولا سديس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: My she-camel, O my she-camel, neither four years old nor six

poem0041_sa.text_ar: وصليني لا بيتي من ورا هاك الطعوس
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Connect me to my home beyond those sand dunes
poem0041_sa.imagery_tags: sand dunes, home, distance, desert landscape

poem0041_sa.text_ar: السلام عليكم ورحمة الله
poem0041_sa.translation_en: Peace be upon you and God's mercy

poem0063_om.translation_en: From my mind I ask you about knowledge and news

poem0063_om.text_ar: ومن راسي انشدكم بالعلوم والأخبار لأن منكم وفيكم
poem0063_om.translation_en: Because from you and within you / Yes from us and within us
I didn't come to remind or repeat poetry

poem0063_om.text_ar: ولا جيت للتذكير أو شعر للتكرار
poem0063_om.translation_en: But I said to reinforce an idea that elevates us
In the Year of Zayed, goodness rejoices / And the righteous, our poems sprout and our songs bloom
poem0063_om.imagery_tags: Year of Zayed, poems blooming, songs

poem0063_om.translation_en: From Oman, mother of the loyal, came her righteous son
As long as we are from her, we pride ourselves in our names

poem0063_om.text_ar: من عمان أم الأوفياء جاء ولدها البار
poem0063_om.translation_en: My country has the two swords and the cutting dagger
It severs the head that thinks to harm her
From the land of Qaboos whose hand is generous

poem0063_om.text_ar: تمصر به الراس الذي ظن يأذيها
poem0063_om.translation_en: You know him when your eyes see our lands

poem0063_om.text_ar: من ديار قابوس الذي كفه المدرار
poem0063_om.translation_en: To the land of dignity, Zayed's chosen jewel
Emirates, and the name's definition suffices us
To the house of Khalifa who fathered a mighty leader
His face, when seeing the nation, says 'revive us'

poem0063_om.text_ar: لدار الخليفة اللي ولد قائد جرار
poem0063_om.translation_en: A nation whose falcon, when it rises, ignites the fire's fuse

poem0063_om.text_ar: وله وجه لا شاف الوطن قال يحيينا
poem0063_om.translation_en: Aggression retreats from her, may God protect us

poem0063_om.text_ar: بلاد صقرها ان فز خاشر فتيل النار
poem0063_om.translation_en: November 18th they bring flowers, we find the entrances calling us with joy
December 2nd we decorate with lights, we have a holiday that concerns you, you have a holiday that concerns us

poem0063_om.text_ar: ثمانية عشر نوفمبر تسوقوا له الأزهار نلاقي المنافذ من فرحها تنادينا
poem0063_om.translation_en: Mohammed bin Rashid initiates and we don't hesitate, he says victory is one, establishing our principles

poem0063_om.text_ar: واثنين ديسمبر نزيّن له الأنوار لنا عيد يعنيكم، لكم عيد يعنينا
poem0063_om.translation_en: And when Mohammed bin Zayed the brave blesses

poem0063_om.text_ar: ومحمد بن راشد يبادر ولا نحتار يقول النصر واحد يأصّل مبادينا
poem0063_om.translation_en: Be assured he honors us in our congratulations

poem0063_om.text_ar: وإذا بارك محمد بن زايد المغوار
poem0063_om.translation_en: We have blood in the tribes' veins dyed free
Be assured that he has honored us in our congratulations

poem0063_om.text_ar: لنا دم في عروق القبائل صبغ أحرار
poem0063_om.translation_en: We have blood in the veins of tribes that dyed the free ones
It binds in our present with the covenant of our past

poem0063_om.text_ar: ومن قال هذه دار والثانية هي دار
poem0063_om.translation_en: And whoever said this is a home and the other is a home

poem0063_om.text_ar: أقول الجسد واحد و ذيلا أيادينا
poem0063_om.translation_en: I say the body is one and these are our hands

poem0063_om.text_ar: ومن قال طيب جار ومجاورٍ له جار
poem0063_om.translation_en: And whoever says a good neighbor and one who neighbors him is a neighbor
I tell him one verse
And by it we were raised
And when you see the boundaries of our lands
There is no wall upon it
So know that we will protect it
And she will protect us
So know that we will protect it
And she will protect us
Our relationships grow
And we remain with great minds
And when we hear of separation
Our ears laugh together

poem0063_om.text_ar: وإن شفنا سايق فتنة نقبضه الدوّار
poem0063_om.translation_en: And if we see someone driving sedition, we arrest him at the roundabout

poem0063_om.text_ar: ويرجع من الفتنة حذر من خطاوينا
poem0063_om.translation_en: And he returns from the discord, wary of our missteps

poem0063_om.text_ar: ومن فكر في تأويل أو حيرته أشوار
poem0063_om.translation_en: And whoever contemplates interpretation or is confused by thorns/difficulties
He looks at our chairs to know our anchors/moorings

poem0063_om.text_ar: قفض عمره قصيدي وسلم سلامٍ حار
poem0063_om.translation_en: My poem ended its life and delivered a burning greeting

poem0063_om.text_ar: بعد ما رسل أمواج حبه إلى الإمارات
poem0063_om.translation_en: After he sent waves of his love to the Emirates

poem0072_ku.text_ar: بعد غابت نجوم السماء من يقدّيني عن البال
poem0072_ku.translation_en: After the stars of the sky disappeared, who will guide me from thoughts
What prevents you, O bitter visitor

poem0072_ku.text_ar: تمر ان بغيت تمر من بيني وبيني
poem0072_ku.translation_en: Pass if you wish to pass between me and myself

poem0072_ku.text_ar: وانا كل ما ناح الحمام بضمير وطار
poem0072_ku.translation_en: And I, whenever the dove coos with conscience and flies
I wished my hands could be
My wings, my escape in my memory
From facing destinies
Repeating in my daily life
And became my routine
I roam the streets
And the streets roam the house
And the stillness of homes
Moves what dwells within me
From my hands, time took her with its hands
And turned, and my best years were lost with it

poem0072_ku.text_ar: من يديني يا زوار خلوني على البال
poem0072_ku.translation_en: Who will guide me, O visitors, leave me in thoughts

poem0072_ku.text_ar: يا زوار لو أن الوجود في بالها ما يكفيني
poem0072_ku.translation_en: O visitors, if existence in her thoughts isn't enough for me
I became strange in my ways though I'm not strange of ways
And I became in our nights, no longer reaching me

poem0072_ku.text_ar: مثل من دخل غيبوبة وما درى وش صار
poem0072_ku.translation_en: Like one who entered a coma and didn't know what happened

poem0072_ku.text_ar: وبعد ما انتهت غيبوبته قال أنا ويني
poem0072_ku.translation_en: And after his coma ended, he said where am I

poem0072_ku.text_ar: مثل من دخل غيبوبة وما درى وش صار
poem0072_ku.translation_en: Like one who entered a coma and didn't know what happened
And after his coma ended
He said where am I, alone
And my mind filled with thoughts
And I am confused
A thought that makes me patient and a thought
It pleases me, upon her
And there is nothing between me and her
A journey of staying awake through the night
From my withered eyelid
For my eyes, but the circumstances
The one who hinders the union
Many times they provoked me to silence
Her loneliness makes me
That's why I accepted the separation
Without excuses, I bid her farewell
It's as if it means nothing to me
And she left, and the nights were harsh to her
And I am collapsing
She wants someone to remember her
And I want someone who makes me forget

poem0071_ku.text_ar: تسريني الذكرى ولا أدري طريقي وين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: Memories please me but I don't know where my path leads

poem0071_ku.text_ar: متاهة شقاء ما بين موتي وميلادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: A maze of suffering between my death and my birth
Certainty sleeps while doubt and speculation stay awake
And I search for myself within myself, while I'm leaving

poem0071_ku.text_ar: وانا غادي احس اني احتاج اختلي وانقسم نصفين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: I feel I need to be alone and split into two halves
Here is Rajeh the troublesome, here is Rajeh the peaceful
At the table we sit together
And we understand each other until I determine my fate and can free my leadership
I am two and suffer intensely from both who have two positions, and my position toward them is neutral
The first says be content and don't follow the locked ones, the other says return and don't fail your caller
Their beloved solitude has brought me shame, and there's nothing from great passion to guide
They say the wound's depth is from the knife's sharpness, they didn't tell me the deepest wound is my wound from my bandage
I became a stranger in the exile of your separation for three years, and I don't know the reason for my exile, oh cause of my insomnia

poem0071_ku.text_ar: الأول يقول اقنع ولا تتبع المقفين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: My chest is a wall for your weeping, not made of clay, and I won't accept any land but you, oh land of my appointment

poem0071_ku.text_ar: والآخر يقول ارجع ولا تخذل منادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: After I sold my laughter, I bought you with a tear, I wept for you though I wasn't unjust nor the initiator
Men aren't diminished by tears for the precious ones, whoever parts from loved ones and weeps is normal

poem0071_ku.text_ar: ولا يوجد اشياء من عظيم الوله
poem0071_ku.translation_en: The singer consoled me before we parted, now I search for solace but the singer doesn't sing

poem0071_ku.text_ar: يقولون عمق الجرح من حدة السكين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: I entertained you after hope vanished in later hope, I came with my heart in hand and my stubbornness behind me

poem0071_ku.text_ar: ما قالوا لي أعمق جرح جرحي من ضمادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: At the valley's crossroads I stood waiting for you to come, but love's wind didn't blow from the valley's crossroads
poem0071_ku.imagery_tags: valley crossroads, waiting, wind of love

poem0071_ku.text_ar: يقولون عمق الجرح من حدة السكين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: Peace
They didn't tell me about the deepest wound
My wound is from my own bandage
I became estranged in the exile of your separation
Three years
And I don't know the reason for my exile
Oh curse of my sleeplessness
And my chest is a wall for your tears
He is not made of clay
And I will not accept any land other than you
O land of my appointment/promise
After I sold my laughter
I bought you with a tear from my eye
I cried for you
And I was not an oppressor

poem0071_ku.text_ar: ولا بادي ولا ينقص الرجال دمعه على الغالين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: And tears do not diminish a man's worth when shed for beloved ones

poem0071_ku.text_ar: من مفارق الغالين رجل وبكى، عادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: A man who parted from loved ones and wept, it's normal

poem0071_ku.text_ar: ولا ينقص الرجال دمعه على الغالين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: And tears for beloved ones do not diminish men

poem0071_ku.text_ar: من مفارق الغالين رجل وبكى، عادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: A man who parted from loved ones wept, it's normal
The singer consoles me before we part
And now I search for solace
And when the singer sang, I walked to you

poem0071_ku.text_ar: سريتك بعد غاب الأمل في رجاء بعدين
poem0071_ku.translation_en: I walked to you after hope vanished in the expectation of distant ones

poem0071_ku.text_ar: وجيت بيدي قلبي ومن خلفي عنادي على مفرق الوادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: I came with my heart in my hand and my stubbornness behind me at the valley's crossroads

poem0071_ku.text_ar: وقفت أحتريك تجين لكن الهوى ما هب من مفرق الوادي
poem0071_ku.translation_en: I stood waiting for you to come, but the wind of love did not blow from the valley's divide
Peace

poem_new_01.translation_en: Oh my friend, there's no need for disputes when you impose your greed before me
Look at your right hand, what have you offered? My feelings are not public transport
To gather your talk and make your mistakes
Last year I said you had long arms, today I withdraw my words and challenge you
I defended you but my defense didn't benefit me, I elevated you and gave you value
I elevated your status, and since all we did was wasted, we ask nothing but your separation after exposing you
Oh you of bad endeavors, you have no duty to us except what came to you, I turned away from you
The time for farewell has come, the lesson is your lesson and the sins are your sins
Today I made you an artificial bump, I pass while you object and I surpass you
We have stepped on the heads of serpents, so how would we stop for the likes of you

poem_new_01.text_ar: وكبرت مركاك ومدام كل اللي فعلناه
poem_new_01.translation_en: And your ship grew larger, and all that we did remains
Lost, there is nothing we ask for
Nothing but your separation after I exposed you

poem_new_01.text_ar: يا ردي المساعي مالك علينا واجب غير ما جاك صديت عنك
poem_new_01.translation_en: O you of poor efforts, you have no duty towards us except what came to you, I turned away from you

poem_new_01.text_ar: وحان وقت الوداع والدرس درسك والخطايا خطاياك
poem_new_01.translation_en: The time of farewell has come, the lesson is your lesson and the sins are your sins

poem_new_01.text_ar: اليوم خليتك مطبٍ صناعي أمر وأنت معترض واتعداك
poem_new_01.translation_en: Today I made you an artificial doctor, I command and you object and I pass you by
poem_new_01.imagery_tags: artificial doctor, command, objection, passing by

poem_new_01.text_ar: وحنا وطينا فوق روس الأفاعي وشلون ناقف عند مثلك وشرواك
poem_new_01.translation_en: We have stepped upon the heads of serpents, so how would we stop for the likes of you and your kind

poem0007_em.text_ar: يقول مغرور متكبر وشايف نفسك وأنا الغريم الي يموت بصوتك عوّدت عيني أن تنام بحسك والي قد نامت على جبروتك بغيت أثيرك بالهوى وأجسك لكن ذبحني يا الحبيب سكوتك. تدري بحبك في العمر متمسك يا لابس درك على ياقوتك أخاف لا لفح النسيم واقول عل موتي فدا
poem0007_em.translation_en: Arrogant and proud, seeing yourself / Away from your death, taken from people's eyes

poem0007_em.text_ar: عن موتك وآخذك عن عين البشر
poem0007_em.translation_en: And I hide you between my ribs / Alone in your homes, you know
Upon my heart your seedling has grown / A plant distinguished by its cherries
And your berries, by God, O branch of youth / What's wrong with you? Flutter with me
Before the young man's age passes you by / My feelings scream
Calling your senses and saying / Let me hear your voice for a minute
I beseech you by God, O gentle morning breeze
What's wrong with you? Be gentle with me
Before the youth's life passes you by
She calls out to your feelings and says
Let me hear your voice for a minute

poem0010_em.text_ar: يقول يا الغلا يا ولد الأيوادي يا محبب فيك خلق الله صوتك الهادي على فوادي مثل نسمة ريف ومطلة
poem0010_em.translation_en: He says, O beloved, O son of nobles, O you in whom God's creation is beloved, your gentle voice upon my heart is like a countryside breeze and rain

poem0010_em.text_ar: وفي حديثك همس متبادي ملحشة يبري ألف علة مثل همس الورد للنادي قبل نور الشمس ليشلة
poem0010_em.translation_en: In your speech is a whisper that appears, a balm that heals a thousand ailments, like the rose's whisper to the dew before the sun's light carries it away
Or like a gentle string melody played by an artist who is passionate
Or like the song of a singing nightingale, the listener, by God, never tires of it

poem0010_em.text_ar: او كغنوة قنطر شادي المستمع والله ما مله
poem0010_em.translation_en: Emotions are led by you, wherever the right one falls, it becomes lost
In your words is truth and certainty, and everything about you is in its beauty
I await your usual appointment like one upon a burning fire

poem0010_em.text_ar: في كلامك صدق ووكادي وكل شيء فيك
poem0010_em.translation_en: My eyelids are deprived of sleep, suffering my condition, let it be

poem0010_em.text_ar: في حله، انتظر ميعادك العادي مثل الي على نار مشعلة
poem0010_em.translation_en: I imagine you as a gazelle in a valley, afraid the hunter will ambush it

poem0010_em.text_ar: منحرم جفني من رقادي مستحسف حالتي
poem0010_em.translation_en: Even if I were skilled and a hunter, that gazelle is difficult for me to reach

poem0010_em.text_ar: خله، أستخيلك ريم في وادي خايف القناص
poem0010_em.translation_en: And forgiveness for my past poems, I haven't revealed all that's hidden

poem0010_em.text_ar: يختله، لو أنا ماهر وصيادي صعب ذاك الريم
poem0010_em.translation_en: How it passed and the beginning ended, intending to occupy a valley

poem0010_em.text_ar: أوصل له، والعفو عن سالف أنشادي
poem0010_em.translation_en: O beloved, O son of nobles, O you in whom God's creation is beloved, your gentle voice upon my heart is like a countryside breeze and rain

poem0010_em.text_ar: ما فضيت المختفي كله، كيف جازه
poem0010_em.translation_en: I haven't revealed all that is hidden, how did it pass through
And the Bedouin ends up intending to settle in the valley

poem0010_em.text_ar: بتحتله يا الغلا يا ولد الأجوادي يا محبب فيك
poem0010_em.translation_en: I will occupy it, O precious one, O son of noble ones, O beloved in you
