poem0010_em.text_ar: خلق الله، صوتك الهادي على فوادي
poem0010_em.translation_en: God's creation, your gentle voice upon my heart
Like a countryside breeze and a view

poem0005_em.text_ar:
تعال علمني الهوى بالتفاصيل
مادام عندك بالتفاصيل خبرة
أنا ظلام في الهوى وأنت قنديل
خل الفجر يغتاظ منك ويشره
من دون نورك كيف أنا بغسل الليل
من ظلمته ويبين للعين فجره 
انثر من دروسك لدربي أكاليل
وأنا بعلقها على صدر بكرة
ومادمت عصفورك واغني مواويل
وانته لعصفور المحبين سدرة
دعني أحلّق فوق روس ال
poem0005_em.translation_en: Come teach me love in all its details
And I'll harvest you, O branch of feelings, the crops

poem0005_em.text_ar: ثمرة سر الهوى يحتاج أحيان تحليل
poem0005_em.translation_en: The fruit of love's secret sometimes needs analysis
And please give me an idea

poem0005_em.text_ar: عن لا تخليني على جاري السيل
poem0005_em.translation_en: About not leaving me by the flowing stream

poem0005_em.text_ar: ظامي ولا اقدر ارتشف منك قطرة
poem0005_em.translation_en: Thirsty and unable to sip even a drop from you

poem0005_em.text_ar: معك منديل وان شئت مررني على خد زهرة
poem0005_em.translation_en: With you as a handkerchief, and if you wish, pass me over a flower's cheek
And when you wake me with the chime of anklets
I die and find in you a glance of contentment

poem0004_em.translation_en: He says when she calls with the voice of the frightened one / Upon her with the same I call my voice
And I answer her with hidden melodies / And the moans of the passionate one in my solitude
And I converse with her while God's creation is at peace / And I fight from weakness the pleasures of my strength
My tears make streams upon the cheek / Like the stream of the sea with the fishing boat
And the heartbeats become strong / And I fear the silent chest will explode

And I forgive what the beloved has done to me, let the blamers and gloaters whisper about me / Even if he cut off his affection for me and betrayed a firm covenant and treated me harshly like one treats a riding camel whose owner cuts it off from pasture
By God, I will not betray him as a brother, upon what is between us I must remain silent / Even if I intended to distance myself a little from him, towards him my steps return me
My beloved whose goodness has overflowed upon me / He possessed me and I am willingly his
I conceal while love is internal / Pure in the hidden of the hidden, my passing, for him from me as long as the soul lives, the meanings of love my verses carry
And for him my soul upon my palm as a gift / And I obey him if the beloved desires my death

poem0004_em.text_ar: وأبيّح ما جنى المحبوب فيّه ترمس بي معاذيل وشموتي
poem0004_em.translation_en: And for him from me peace and greetings / As many times as I have moaned or called my voice

poem0004_em.text_ar: ولو خلي قطع في الحب ريّه علي وخان ميثاقٍ ثبوتي وعسفني مثلما عسف المطيّة وصاحبها يقطعها المروتي
poem0004_em.translation_en: Even if he cut off his affection in love towards me, betrayed a firm covenant, and oppressed me like one oppresses a riding camel whose owner cuts through the desert paths
poem0004_em.imagery_tags: desert journey, camel riding, broken covenant

poem0004_em.text_ar: فلا والله ما اخونه خويّه على مابيننا بلزم سكوتي ولو اني نويت ابعد شويّه عنه.. صوبه ترجّعني خطوت
poem0004_em.translation_en: By God, I would never betray my brother, about what's between us I must keep silent, and even if I intend to distance myself a little from him, my steps bring me back to him
My beloved who bestowed his goodness upon me
He possessed me and for him I submitted my pride
I conceal while love is deep within me
poem0004_em.imagery_tags: hidden feelings, inner fire, masked heart

poem0004_em.translation_en: Pure in the hidden of the hidden
You pass by, and for her I have eternal wine

poem0004_em.text_ar: الروح حية معاني الحب تحملها بيوتي
poem0004_em.translation_en: The soul is alive, carrying the meanings of love in my verses
And my soul is for him, a gift upon my palm
And I obey him even if the beloved oppresses
My death, yet from me comes peace and greetings
The number of times I groaned or raised my voice

poem0008_em.text_ar: رحبت بك من قلب شفقان من قلب متولع بطاريك رحبت بك ترحيب ظميان وعن ما خلق ربي أبدّيك يا زين يا مدعوج
poem0008_em.translation_en: I welcomed you from a compassionate heart, from a heart burning with your memories. I welcomed you like a thirsty person welcomes water, and I show you what my Lord has created, O beautiful one with curved eyelashes

poem0008_em.text_ar: الأعيان ما شيء ماشي بعذري كان يرضيك يا زين يا مدعوج الأعيان ما شيء بعذري كان يرضيك طلبت من معبود غفران ربي من أمراضك يشافيك
poem0008_em.translation_en: O beautiful one with curved eyelashes, nothing in my excuse would please you. I asked the Worshipped One for forgiveness, may my Lord heal you from your ailments
May you remain with me throughout all times, living happily with your lover and those who cherish you
O one with soft braids on your back, like the darkness of night covering you, your cheek shines like lightning in the clouds, or like crystal enveloping you
poem0008_em.imagery_tags: braided hair, night darkness, lightning, crystal

poem0008_em.text_ar: وغاليك يا بو جديلٍ على المتن لان
poem0008_em.translation_en: Your love has possessed me like a sultan possesses slaves who are owned. Your affection with me outweighs all measures, I will never forget you throughout my life
poem0008_em.imagery_tags: sultan's possession, slaves, scales of measure

poem0008_em.translation_en: I have made my soul cheap for you without price, for your sake my songs would please you. From you comes forgiveness and we seek your pardon, O you who owns the soul in your hands
No human has truly possessed me other than you, no, by the One who knows your secrets

poem0008_em.text_ar: الأمزان ولا كما البلور غاشيك حبك
poem0008_em.translation_en: Love has covered you like crystal or like the rains
He owned me with the ownership of a sultan who owns slaves
Those who are slaves to your affection with me

poem0008_em.text_ar: ترجح به أوزان طول العمر ماني
poem0008_em.translation_en: My life's weights are swayed by it, I am not
To forget you, I cheapened my soul for you
My songs were for you without any price
It pleases you, and from you comes forgiveness, and we desire
Forgive me, O you who have possessed my soul

poem0008_em.text_ar: بيديك ما جد ملكني غيرك إنسان
poem0008_em.translation_en: In your hands, no other human has truly possessed me
No, by the One who knows your hidden secrets

poem0009_em.translation_en: He says he reproaches me, yet his reproach is gentle
Of noble taste and light blood
He reproaches me and upon my heart his reproach strikes like autumn breezes
I say he's like pearls selected by the captain's hand
No falseness in him, counted for me from those pearls with measure and in ordered rows
And I'm lost in him and his words, living in the world of pure love
His eyes speak letters of love, a gentle address came from them

poem0009_em.text_ar: ما فيه زيف حسب لي من بعض
poem0009_em.translation_en: You reproach me and carry blame for me, while I whose gaze is passionate for her
Her magic affects my heart and soul, creating flutter within my heart
I said please, your reproach is enough, no need for your eyes to add more
Reproach and blame, burdening your lover with the magic of eyes and delicate lashes
You are my life and my song, you possessed the heart as owner and companion
How beautiful is the love between us, pure and carrying noble meaning
After that reproach, he smiled and white pearls appeared clean in his mouth
God's peace upon that smile that contained all meanings for the beloved
He reproaches me yet his reproach is gentle, of noble taste and light blood
He reproaches me and upon my heart his reproach strikes like autumn breezes
The passion of her magic has deeply affected my heart
And my soul settled inside my heart
Rafif, I said I beg you
Your reproach is enough for me, there's no need for your eyes
Why do you add reproach and blame
And bear with your lover's enchanting eyes
And the leafy/shaded eyelash
You are life to me
And I have a singing beauty who has possessed my heart

poem0009_em.text_ar: مالك وليف يا محلا الهوى بيني
poem0009_em.translation_en: You have no companion, oh how beautiful is the love between us
And between you is one who is chaste and carries the meaning
The noble one after that reproach
He smiled at me and the pearls appeared

poem0009_em.text_ar: في ثغره نظيف سلام الله على ذيك
poem0009_em.translation_en: In his smile is pure peace, God's blessings upon that one
The smile contained all meanings
My companion reproaches me
And a beloved one, gentle with exquisite refined taste
And the light-blooded one reproaches me
And upon my heart is his reproach
He snatched the sparks of autumn breezes

poem0002_em.translation_en: Break the wall of silence and speak a little, let the conversation restore the course of my life
I fear with too much silence you might kill the living one who yearns to hear your voice like me
Your silence is darkness where light breaks, and your voice restores light even if dead
If you are cold, see me burned with branding, it's wrong for you to become the cause of my death
As long as you're with me in love's mission, you've shared my soul, taste and self
You became like sustenance for the soul and water, from you is loss and in you I find my wealth
It's wrong to cut irrigation from my heart's planting while it dies as I'm above the Euphrates shore
See me with you as everything, without you nothing but shadow or remnants of my dispersion
Break the wall of silence, speak and speak a little, let the conversation restore the course of my life
And the light breaks before it
And your voice brings back the light even if it was
I will not die, even if you are a file (tool for grinding/wearing down)
Look at me, burned like this
And it's forbidden that you become a cause for me

poem0002_em.text_ar: في مماتي وما دامني وياك في مهمه الغي شاركتني روحي وذوقي وذاتي
poem0002_em.translation_en: In my death and as long as I'm with you in the mission of temptation, my soul, taste and essence have partnered with me

poem0002_em.text_ar: وأصبحت مثل الزاد للروح والمي منك الخسارة وفيك القى غناتي
poem0002_em.translation_en: You have become like sustenance for the soul and water, from you is loss and in you I find my wealth

poem0002_em.text_ar: حرام تقطع عن زرع قلبي الري ويموت وأنا فوق شط الفرات
poem0002_em.translation_en: It's forbidden that you cut off irrigation from my heart's crops while they die and I'm above the banks of the Euphrates

poem0002_em.text_ar: شفني معك كل شيء وبلاك لا شيء إلا خيال أو بقايا شتاتي
poem0002_em.translation_en: I saw with you everything, and without you nothing but illusion or remnants of my dispersion

poem0002_em.text_ar: اكسر جدار الصمت وتحدث وتحدث شوي خل الحديث يعيد مجرى حياتي
poem0002_em.translation_en: Break the wall of silence and speak, speak a little, let the conversation restore the course of my life
poem0002_em.imagery_tags: wall, silence, breaking barriers, flowing river of life

poem0001_em.text_ar: أعرف اللي في قلبك من عيونك
poem0001_em.translation_en: I know what's in your heart from your eyes, my beloved, without your tongue speaking it
When your eyelids flutter over your eyes, they translate for me what your expression hides

poem0001_em.text_ar: إذا رفّت على عينك جفونك تترجم لي بما يخفي بيانك
poem0001_em.translation_en: And if you want to know who loves you, come and look into my eyes

poem0001_em.text_ar: وإذا ودك تعرف اللي يبونك تعال وبرّق في عيني
poem0001_em.translation_en: Look and see the kings of my heart worshipping you when you gesture to me with your fingertips

poem0001_em.text_ar: عيانك وشوف ملوك قلبي يعبدونك إذا أشرت لي بأطرف بنانك
poem0001_em.translation_en: Your eyes witness how the kings of my heart worship you when you gesture to me with your fingertips
poem0001_em.imagery_tags: eyes, kings, heart, worship, fingertips, gesture

poem0006_em.text_ar: أقول على شانك أموت ألفين مرة
poem0006_em.translation_en: I say for your sake I would die two thousand times, And I would sacrifice my soul cheaply for your eyes
If I possessed the kingdom of the universe I would scatter it, And surrender my life into your right hand

poem0006_em.text_ar: وملك الكون لو في يدي أفره
poem0006_em.translation_en: Because you have a precious place settled in my heart, And you have taken me as your captive
Our love you see is continuous, So do not listen to the blame of your critics

poem0006_em.text_ar: لأنه لك غلا ساطي مقره وسط قلبي
poem0006_em.translation_en: And do not sever the rope of affection towards you, The judges of love will condemn you for that
poem0006_em.imagery_tags: rope of affection, judges of love

poem0006_em.translation_en: The cups of passion are sweet and bitter, Drink them with me, may God help you
poem0006_em.imagery_tags: cups of passion, sweet and bitter

poem0006_em.translation_en: And whoever pledged loyalty to you fulfilled it, His eye remains always watching you
With your rejection and abandonment you don't harm him, You show him your harshness after your gentleness
You are to me a pearl among treasures, The maidens envy your beauty and adornment
An Arab from a lineage of free people, Noble, pure, with a clear forehead

poem0006_em.text_ar: ومرة تجرعها معي ربي يعينك واللي عاهدك على الإخلاص
poem0006_em.translation_en: O you who have become the delight of my sight, And a prince in love we consider you
poem0006_em.imagery_tags: delight of sight, prince of love

poem0006_em.translation_en: I beg you to erase the record of sins, And help me, may God not humiliate you

poem0006_em.text_ar: عوينك بصدك والهجر عن لا تضره تبيّن له قساتك عقب لينك
poem0006_em.translation_en: Let love prevail and settle, As long as it remains between me and you

poem0006_em.text_ar: فأنت لي من اليكّات درة تغار الخود من حسنك وزينك
poem0006_em.translation_en: Leave aside reproach and resentment from you, Forgive us as we forgive you

poem0006_em.text_ar: عريب ومن سلالة ناس حرة نزيهٍ طيبٍ صافي جبينك
poem0006_em.translation_en: You are Arab from a lineage of free people, noble and pure with a clear forehead

poem0006_em.text_ar: فيا من للنظر أمسيت قرّة وأميرِ في المحبة
poem0006_em.translation_en: O you who have become the delight of my sight and my prince in love
They consider you a record of sins, I beg you to erase it
And my God helped me, may He not humiliate you
Let love be my guide and settle in it
As long as there remains between me and you
And leave the blame and resentment away from you
And forgive us as we forgive you

poem0003_em.text_ar: تضحك ويغمرني الضحك تبكي تبكيني معاك لو كان تخفي تفضحك عينك وتكشف وش بلاك أرجوك خلي وأنصحك تعطف على من هو هواك من عاش عمره يمنحك حبه ويتمنى رضاك مادام وزنك ير
poem0003_em.translation_en: You laugh and laughter overwhelms me, you cry and I cry with you. Even if you try to hide, your eyes betray you and reveal what troubles you

poem0003_em.text_ar: جحك في الحب عن منهم سواك والناس دايم
poem0003_em.translation_en: I beg you, let me advise you - be gentle with the one who loves you. The one who lived his life granting you his love and wishing for your satisfaction
As long as your worth outweighs all others in love, and people always praise you for your goodness and describe your allure
I forgive and pardon and allow you, and erase our mistakes in your enmity. I lose my life but win you - the world is worthless without you
And forgive, and I forgive you
And erase our mistakes in your enmity
I lose my life and win you
The world is of no use without you

poem0021_em.translation_en: You neglected me and forgot / How could you forget me? You forgot
And you forgot our reunion together / In the evening time at the going home
None would reject the covenant like you / By your hand signed, upon the promise renowned
I didn't say then that I don't want you / If I said I would cheapen you, you died
You want to sprinkle the covenant with oil / And I'll ignite fires at your feet
You'll say 'O Tanaf, you slipped' / The hidden one gloated after you
The keeper of scorpions and toilets, what do you want? The keeper of scorpions and toilets / Doesn't help when approaching you, impure, carrying mountains of sin

poem0021_em.text_ar: ما قلت ذاك الحين ما أباك
poem0021_em.translation_en: Your sin won't be forgiven, God forbid / Even if you wash with sackcloth from Reet, the soul doesn't desire your water from heart to seat
You ruined, your mention fell with me and your ways, unimportant / And I raised my sail away from you, the heart I've cut off and don't want you
You want to sprinkle the covenant with oil
And I ignite fires in your wake
You will say, O Tanaf, I have erred
I rejoiced in secret after you left

poem0021_em.text_ar: راعي العقارب والتواليت ايش تبغي به؟ راعي العقارب والتواليت ما
poem0021_em.translation_en: The keeper of scorpions and misfortunes, what do you want from him? The keeper of scorpions and misfortunes has nothing

poem0021_em.text_ar: مايفيد يوم تقرب حذاك نجس وجبل من الذنب شليت. ما ينغفر ذنبك وحاشاك.
poem0021_em.translation_en: It benefits when you approach while impure, carrying a mountain of sins. Your sin won't be forgiven, God forbid.
poem0021_em.imagery_tags: impurity, mountain of sins, divine forgiveness

poem0021_em.text_ar: لو تغسل بخيش من الريت. النفس ما تهوى هلاماك. من القب لين السيت
poem0021_em.translation_en: Even if you wash with burlap from the sand, the soul does not desire what you have, from the heart to the reputation

poem0021_em.text_ar: خريت. عندي سقط ذكرك وطرواك. مهم. وانا ملا شراعي عنك عليت.
poem0021_em.translation_en: You've fallen from my regard, your mention and memory are no longer important, and I've raised my sail away from you
The heart, I've told its story, and I don't want you

poem0019_em.text_ar: يا نفس كم أنصحچ باطن وتعصين
poem0019_em.translation_en: O soul, how much I've advised you secretly yet you disobey
I say carry from their love only in measure
I fear from excessive love you will melt away
Like copper melts when it melts in the goldsmith's furnace
From their abandonment, I feel two sorrows upon my heart
What can I do with one whose place is on the left side
Between their cheeks are two lightning flashes

poem0019_em.text_ar: وش حيلتي في من محله ياللي اليسار
poem0019_em.translation_en: A nose and features that strike the heart broken

poem0019_em.text_ar: فيه من محله ياللي يسار تراه معلى
poem0019_em.translation_en: In it from his place, O you who are asked, you see him elevated
Between his cheeks are two lightning bolts
A nose and passion that leave the heart broken

poem0023_em.translation_en: I love you and yearn for your presence
I hate and oppose those who oppose you
In the midst of my organs, my heart answers your call
My soul and spirit answer to you
Is there anyone among creation who resembles you? Only Prophet Joseph matches your beauty
Do not extend your right hand to the envious, the envious one will ruin your meanings
People try to seek your satisfaction, while I with my state and wealth will please you
If you come to take what you need, I would even tear out my soul to satisfy you
There are people who try to seek your satisfaction
I am in a state and wealth that pleases you
And if you come to me, you will take your due
Even if I tear my soul apart, I will sue you

poem0024_em.text_ar: يا لتني بالقرب وحذاچ ان كان بوصل ما بعنّيچ
poem0024_em.translation_en: Oh, if only I were close and beside you, even if connection doesn't concern you

poem0024_em.text_ar: ما أنا مريض مريض وشفاي من دواچ
poem0024_em.translation_en: I am not sick, I am sick, and my cure is from your medicine

poem0024_em.text_ar: وأنا طبيب أنا طبيبچ اللي بداويچ
poem0024_em.translation_en: And I am a doctor, I am your doctor who will heal you

poem0020_em.translation_en: Tanaf says in the corridor of the heart / You ask where is the corridor of the heart
In the corridor of the heart I placed you / I said this is a bird of paradise
I thought well of you / I thank God the day I found you
I wanted neither queen nor year / Just enough, a crown upon the head I placed you
And the envious one deceives / With his suspicion, how much suspected
I came to you, told you my heart is torn / How much I took and how much I gave you
And you, the stubborn one, forgetting / The day they told me how much I excused you
How much I excused you and called to you / To stay away from those hostile to me
But enough, enough, I'm tired of you / From your torment that I've endured
If only I had known you / If I had healed you, and the cure I have plenty of
I placed you/I put you down
And the envious one befriends/accompanies
How much does he assume about what is assumed

poem0020_em.text_ar: انا جيتك قلت لك قلبي مشقنه
poem0020_em.translation_en: I came to you and told you my heart is tormented
How much you took and how much I gave you
And you, O persistent one, are our people
The day they told me
How many times I have excused you
How many times I've excused you and whispered to you
You distance yourself from those whose nature is hostile
Enough, enough
And I've just grown tired of you
From your torment I have my share of suffering
If only I had known you
If I have healed you
And the medicine I have is abundant from it

poem0022_em.translation_en: Tanaf says, How much I've run with the wind of my time
Understanding the wanderings and the craft, Reaching seventy of my age
Reaching seventy of my age, And passion still wrestles in the heart
Craft for me from your ember crafting, And serve me chains and shackles
Don't you see the tears pouring down, One has robbed me, wanting me

poem0022_em.text_ar: السبعين من عمري والهوى في القلب
poem0022_em.translation_en: Robbed me of sweet encounters, Wounded me in my chest and placed a brand upon the wound
Who brands over a wound? Placed a brand upon my wound
I was destroyed and came to my senses, I saw relief, I saw relief but reason strayed

poem0022_em.text_ar: غك الجمري واخدم ليّ قيد ورصاغة
poem0022_em.translation_en: I saw a cricket apologizing, Above a bucket and under foliage
I'll strike it with the cobra flowing, Though its spray falls short of its brain
I said this is the day of my purchase, I'll cross, this isn't much of a palatable crossing
We'll cross in a palatable crossing, Leave me now, leave me O cup
From my blood, my deficient branding seeks expression, My dark garment hasn't taken me

poem0022_em.text_ar: سالبني حلو المواغى، جارحنني في وسط صدري وحط فوق الجرح لي داغة
poem0022_em.translation_en: Measure while the oysters are in their evasion

poem0022_em.text_ar: حد يوسم فوق الجرح؟ حط فوق الجرح لي داغة
poem0022_em.translation_en: Does anyone brand over a wound? He placed another burn mark over my wound

poem0022_em.text_ar: انعدمت وجيت ببري شفت يارح شفت يارح والعقل زاغ
poem0022_em.translation_en: I was lost and came to the wilderness, I saw oh wind, I saw oh wind, and my mind went astray

poem0022_em.text_ar: شفت هالجرجور متذري فوق دلخ وتحت نباغا
poem0022_em.translation_en: I saw a cricket and a dragonfly above the muddy ground and below the tobacco plants
poem0022_em.imagery_tags: insects, mud, tobacco plants, rural landscape

poem0022_em.translation_en: With a strike while the bridge is my night path
His effort fell short of reaching his mind
poem0022_em.imagery_tags: mental capacity, cognitive limitation, intellectual shortcoming

poem0022_em.text_ar: قد قصر طشه عن دماغه، قلت هذا اليوم ومشري
poem0022_em.translation_en: I said this day and my path
I will cross/pass through
This is not much of a lesson to swallow
We will cross through a passage that is traversable
Leave me now, it's time
Leave me, O cup/glass
From my destruction, my shortcoming burns, wanting to be conveyed
My youthful garment has not taken me
Measure while the oyster is in its hiding place

poem0022_em.text_ar:
الغدر ماهو بمضطري
لو بحر بموج يتلاغى
ياي عانني ومتعني
انت يايني ولا انا ماييتك
والي يدق الباب يتناغى
poem0022_em.translation_en: Music

poem0070_ku.translation_en: Take forgiveness and rise above people when they err
And whoever thinks ill of you, leave him to his suspicion
By God, those who think ill only harm themselves
He nurtures a claw in his chest and gets wounded by it
Don't let improving the thoughts of those who suspect occupy you
People don't hold the keys to heaven and hell

poem0070_ku.text_ar: يربي في صدره مخلب وينجرح منه
poem0070_ku.translation_en: You shouldn't abandon your work to fix someone's bad opinion of you
Frankly, you don't have time for that
Whoever meets me will be blessed with happiness from those I have rights over - well done!

poem0070_ku.text_ar: ترى الناس ما بيديهم النار والجنة
poem0070_ku.translation_en: As for the rest, let them judge as they will - well done!

poem0070_ku.text_ar: ما يصير انت يا رجال تخلي اشغالك
poem0070_ku.translation_en: A famous person is naturally exposed to both good and bad, to gossip and talk
You go to see this one

poem0070_ku.text_ar: ظن فيك ظن سيئ تروح تصلحه
poem0070_ku.translation_en: A bad assumption about you, you go to fix it
You honestly don't have time

poem0070_ku.text_ar: من قابلني يبشر بسعده من اللي عليهم لي حق
poem0070_ku.translation_en: Whoever meets me will be blessed with happiness from those who owe me a right
Worthy/Equal/Match

poem0070_ku.text_ar: ولا الناس الباقين اللي يحكم يحكم
poem0070_ku.translation_en: And not the remaining people, whoever rules will rule
Well done, well done

poem0070_ku.text_ar: الرجال المشهور الإنسان عموما معرض للزين والشين
poem0070_ku.translation_en: The famous man, the human being in general, is exposed to both good and bad
Being exposed to gossip and rumors is natural

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قالو لك الورد ما يجرح ترى يكذبون
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you roses don't wound, truly they lie / Roses are knives when they come before farewell

poem0068_ku.text_ar: الورد سكين لا من جاك قبل الوداع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you kindness doesn't benefit, truly they lie / Kindness benefits when you're not seeking gain

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قالو لك الورد ما يجرح ترى يكذبون
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you fear saves, tell them you lie / How many cowards died and how many brave ones lived

poem0068_ku.text_ar: الورد سكين لا من جاك قبل الوداع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you narrowness is the grave's tightness, say you lie / How many graves are spacious and wide for their inhabitants

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قالو لك الطيب ما ينفع ترى يكذبون
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you isolation hardens, truly they lie / Isolation teaches you the value of gathering

poem0068_ku.text_ar: الطيب ينفع إذا ما كنت راج انتفاع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you people are treacherous, say you lie / Only the bad one whose evil face wears a mask
They told you light is sunlight, say you lie / Light is the light of hearts without rays

poem0068_ku.text_ar: ترى يكذبون الطيب ينفع إذا ما كنت راجي
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you despair kills, tell them you lie / Despair is the first hope at the last station of loss
They told you descending is shameful, say you lie / When a Muslim prostrates to Allah, elevation begins

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل لهم تكذبون يا كم مات الجبان وكم عاش الشجاع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you the soul hates, tell them you lie / The soul is from my Lord's command, it doesn't love conflict

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قالوا لك الضيق ضيق القبر قل تكذبون يا كم قبور على هلها كبار ووساع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you religion is vast, tell them you lie / Religion is two things: monotheism and following

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قالوا لك العزلة تقسي ترى يكذبون العزلة تعلمك وش قيمة الاجتماع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you life is once, tell them you lie / Life is many times when your remembrance spreads
They told you the sea betrays, tell them you lie / The sea has no business with wind or sails

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل تكذبون بس الردي من ردى وجهه لبس له قناع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you profit is buying and selling, they lie / I know things that are bought but not for sale
They told you money enriches, tell them you lie / How many are rich in money but poor in contentment

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل تكذبون بس الردي من ردى وجهه لبس له قناع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: They told you love is a lie, tell them you lie / Love exists but people are deceitful
They told you the light is the light of the sun

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل تكذبون النور نور القلوب لي بليا
poem0068_ku.translation_en: Say you deny the light, the light of hearts is my affliction
A ray of light, they told you that despair is a killer
Tell them you are lying

poem0068_ku.text_ar: الياس أول أمل في آخر محطة ضياع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: Despair is the first hope at the last station of loss
They told you that descending brings shame

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل تكذبون المسلم ليا سجد لله
poem0068_ku.translation_en: Say you deny the Muslim when he prostrates to Allah
The rise has begun
They told you that the soul hates

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل لهم تكذبون الروح من أمر ربي ما تحب الصراع
poem0068_ku.translation_en: Tell them you deny the soul which is from my Lord's command, it does not love conflict
They told you the debt is vast
He said to them: You deny the religion
Two in monotheism/unity
And the followers told you life is but once
Tell them you lie to life repeatedly

poem0068_ku.text_ar: لا من صار ذكرك يذاع قالوا لك العمر مرة
poem0068_ku.translation_en: When your name became widely spoken, they told you life is but once
Tell them you lie to life repeatedly

poem0068_ku.text_ar: لا من صار ذكرك يذاع قالوا لك البحر يغدر
poem0068_ku.translation_en: When your name became widely spoken, they told you the sea betrays

poem0068_ku.text_ar: قل لهم تكذبون البحر ماله شغل في الريح
poem0068_ku.translation_en: Tell them you lie, the sea has no business with the wind

poem0068_ku.text_ar: ولا الشراع قالوا لك الربح بيع ومشترى
poem0068_ku.translation_en: And the sail did not tell you that profit is in buying and selling
They lie
I know things that can be bought
But it's not
Be careful, they told you the sea betrays
Tell them you are denying the sea
He has no business with the wind
And not the sail, they told you about the money
He sings/enriches
Tell them you are lying
How many wealthy people of money

poem0068_ku.text_ar: لكنهم فقارى اقتناع قالوا لك الحب
poem0068_ku.translation_en: But they are poor in conviction, they told you about love
A lie
Tell them you are lying
Love exists
But people in it are deceitful

poem0069_ku.text_ar:
يقول عزت علي نفسي ونفسي عزيزة  
عزت علي نفسي ونفسي عزيزة
 ماهيب ترضى موقف الذل والهون 
الله جعل فيها الكرامة غريزة
يا اكون في راس الهرم ولا ما اكون
والعمر مايسوى بلا عزّ بيزه
حتى ولو في البنك مليون ومليون
وترى الفخر يمنى الفتى ماتحيزه لا صار مايقدم
poem0069_ku.translation_en: My soul is precious to me and my soul is noble, it does not accept positions of humiliation and disgrace, God has made dignity an instinct within it

poem0069_ku.text_ar: وغيره يردون والطيب في الطيب من الله ميزة
poem0069_ku.translation_en: Others may want it, but goodness in the good is a distinction from God
The vultures remain vultures no matter what they do

poem0069_ku.text_ar: واللي مراجلهم لفترة وجيزة يستغفلون الناس والناس يدرون
poem0069_ku.translation_en: And those whose manhood is brief, they try to fool people but people know

poem0048_sa.text_ar: على الهون يا جرح المفارق وضيق البال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: Take it easy, O wound of separation and distress of mind / Take it easy, O sorrow that has overpowered me with its might

poem0048_sa.text_ar: على الهون يا همٍ من سطى بي وقدرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I honor you even as a wound whose struggle is deadly / And I've trained my body for your sickness and made it patient

poem0048_sa.text_ar: أعزك لو انك جرح ومجاهدك
poem0048_sa.translation_en: For the one who possessed my heart from journey to journey / And I opened for them a door that I had closed to others

poem0048_sa.text_ar: قتّال، وروّضت جسمي لأجل سقمك
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I placed shade for them from the scorching sun of passion / And built them a house in my heart and maintained it

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وصبرته لأجل من ملك قلبي
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I preserved them, pampered them, and sang them songs / And chose their nearness - if only I hadn't chosen it

poem0048_sa.text_ar: من الجال لين الجال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: They grazed the meadow of my heart until the grass became barren / And from its barrenness I kept others away

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وفجيت له بابٍ عن الناس سكرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I protect them from anger and anger the critics / Their faults, if great in others' eyes, I minimize

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وحطيت له عن شمس قيظ الغرام
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I gave them pure water of passion and love / After they drew from my heart's blood

poem0048_sa.text_ar: ظلال وشيّدت له بيتٍ بقلبي
poem0048_sa.translation_en: Before night covered them with a shawl / I stole sleep from their eyes and kept them awake

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وعمرته حفظته ودللته وغنيت له موال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I engraved every letter of mine on the chain / They boasted with it and angered those who envied

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وتخيرت قربه ليتني ما تخيرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: After all this appreciation, love and reverence / They changed and their whims overcame them

poem0048_sa.text_ar: حفظته ودللته وغنيت له موال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: They multiplied against me - may God judge them / While I gave them my life without counting

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وتخيّرت قربه ليتني ما تخيرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I dressed them in arrogance until they came strutting / Growing in their own eyes when illusions deceived them

poem0048_sa.text_ar: رعى ريف قلبي لين صار الحشم مدهال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: If it's all jokes and child's play / I shook the branch until the bird flew away

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وعن مدهله جنّبت غيره وذيّرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: We finished and locked the boxes with locks / And from their unjust rule, I freed my heart completely

poem0048_sa.text_ar: أداري عليه من الزعل وأزعل العذال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I conceal my anger from him while angering the blamers

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وخطاه إن كبر في عين غيري
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And his steps, even if they seem great in others' eyes

poem0048_sa.text_ar: تصاغرته سقيته من عدود الغلا والغرام زلال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I belittled him, yet gave him to drink from the wells of passion and love's pure water

poem0048_sa.text_ar: بعد ما جذب من دم قلبي وصدرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: After he drew from my heart's blood and expelled it

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وقبل لا يغطي ليله المنتثر بالشال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And before the night covers the scattered one with a shawl

poem0048_sa.text_ar: سرقت الكرى من نون عينه وسهرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I stole sleep from the pupil of his eye and kept him awake

poem0048_sa.text_ar: ونقش كل حرف من حروفي على السلسال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And I engraved every letter of my letters on the chain

poem0048_sa.text_ar: تباهى بها وأغاظ ناسٍ مجاكرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: He boasted with it and angered people who confronted him

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وبعد كل هالتقدير والحب والإجلال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And after all this appreciation, love, and reverence

poem0048_sa.text_ar: تغير و هقواته عليه مغيرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: He changed and his desires have overcome him

poem0048_sa.text_ar: تكاثر علي الله يجازيه كيف الحال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: May God reward whoever asks me frequently how I am

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وأنا يوم أسوق العمر له ما تكاثرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And when I drive my life towards him, I do not multiply it

poem0048_sa.text_ar: تكاثر علي الله يجازيه كيف الحال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: May God reward whoever asks me frequently 'how are you?'

poem0048_sa.text_ar: وأنا يوم أسوق العمر له ما تكاثرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And when I drive my life towards him, I do not multiply it

poem0048_sa.text_ar: لبسته ثياب الغطرسة لين جاء يختال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: He wore the garments of arrogance until he came strutting

poem0048_sa.text_ar: و كبر في عيونه يوم الأوهام غررته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And he grew arrogant in his eyes when illusions deceived him

poem0048_sa.text_ar: و إذا السالفة ضحك و مهازل
poem0048_sa.translation_en: And if the matter becomes laughter and jokes

poem0048_sa.text_ar: مهازل ولعب عيال
poem0048_sa.translation_en: Jokes and children's play

poem0048_sa.text_ar: خبطت الغصن
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I struck the branch

poem0048_sa.text_ar: لين أبرق الريش طيرته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: Until the feather flashed and made it fly

poem0048_sa.text_ar: خلصنا وقفلنا الصناديق
poem0048_sa.translation_en: We finished and closed the boxes

poem0048_sa.text_ar: بالأقفال ومن جور حكمه كامل
poem0048_sa.translation_en: With locks and from the tyranny of his complete judgment

poem0048_sa.text_ar: القلب حررته
poem0048_sa.translation_en: I freed the heart

poem0049_sa.text_ar: يدور الوقت وتبين لنا الأيام سر الغيب وتظهر هيئة المكتوب من رسمه وعنوانه لزمنا الصمت يوم الصمت في بعض المواقف طيب، قفلنا دون وجه الشر بالمفتاح بيبانه، تخطفنا يدين العبث والتزوير والتخريب، يبون يفرقون الشمل ويهدون جدرانه سعوا للنيل منا بالخفا
poem0049_sa.translation_en: Time revolves and days reveal to us the secret of the unseen, showing the form of what is written from its drawing and title

poem0049_sa.text_ar: فوق بالظلم والتكذيب علينا كل واحد حد سكينه ونيبانه
poem0049_sa.translation_en: We were bound to silence when silence in some situations is good, we locked the door against evil with its key
The hands of chaos, forgery and destruction snatch us, they want to divide unity and demolish its walls

poem0049_sa.text_ar: ما صاب ولا وصلنا منه باروده ودخانه
poem0049_sa.translation_en: They sought to harm us secretly with injustice and lies, each one sharpening his knife and fangs against us

poem0054_om.text_ar: هداني الوقت غادة وألماس لما انجرح رمشي هدتني هدبها شاعر أدور بعاطفة طفلة احساس تصرخ بوسط الناس ضايع ذهبها آمالها أكبر وأكبر من الباس وأكبر من العذراء ومن هو خطبها وأكبر من القمة وأكبر من الكاس وأكبر من نفو
روس تنام بغضبها ما ورّق الراعي سوى صوت الأجراس
poem0054_om.translation_en: The shepherd only rustled with the sound of bells / When these souls swayed to their Arabs
The comfortable one didn't stand for the rest of hardship / The day when beards fear the dignity of its mustache

poem0054_om.text_ar: ما ورّق الراعي سوى صوت الأجراس لما تهادت هالنفوس لعربها ما وقّف المرتاح ترويحة الباس
poem0054_om.translation_en: The seed didn't turn on the roaster's head / Except for the fire to love its flame
Every bearded garment is worn by the kind-hearted / And every soul takes from time what pleases it

poem0054_om.text_ar: وما دارت الحبة على راس محماس
poem0054_om.translation_en: How many girls stay awake without sleep passing by / How many girls sleep came to and overcame
How many girls stay awake without sleep passing by / How many girls sleep came to and overcame

poem0054_om.text_ar: كل ثوب لحية طيب القلب لباس
poem0054_om.translation_en: Yesterday's whispers of nightmares didn't pass / Except to awaken, to leaf through, to pick grapes from my eyes

poem0054_om.text_ar: وكل نفس تاخذ بالزمن ما عجبها
poem0054_om.translation_en: The poet who was visited by a thousand obsessions saw the strange girl whose manners diminished from that day / And my heart's rights with people and her rights, I don't know who stole them

poem0054_om.text_ar: كم طفلة تسهر ولا مرها نعاس
poem0054_om.translation_en: How many young girls stay awake without feeling sleepy
How many little girls has sleep come to and overcome

poem0054_om.text_ar: كم طفلة تسهر ولا مرها نعاس
poem0054_om.translation_en: How many young girls stay awake without feeling sleepy
How many little girls has sleep come to and overcome
Last night, the whispers of Firnas did not pass by
Will you not awaken, will you not bloom from
Do not pluck the grapes from my eyes

poem0054_om.text_ar: الشاعر اللي مر به ألف وسواس شاف الغريبة بنت قلت أدبها من يومها
poem0054_om.translation_en: The poet who was visited by a thousand obsessions saw the strange girl whose manners diminished from that day

poem0054_om.text_ar: وحقوق قلبي مع الناس وحقوقها مدري من اللي سلبها
poem0054_om.translation_en: And the rights of my heart with people and their rights, I don't know who stole them

poem0039_sa.text_ar: وكّل أمرك للكريم ويسمح أمرك كل مادكت على بالك روابع وافرد همومك على سجاد صبرك لا تضيقها وحلم الله واسع واعرف اسباب الردى والطيب وادره واحسب حسابك من الليلة وطالع لا تطاول في قصير الشبر شبرك
poem0039_sa.translation_en: Entrust your affairs to the Generous One and He will ease your matters, whenever troubles weigh upon your mind

poem0039_sa.text_ar: ولا تنازل عن شيمك ولا تراجع ولا تسمع باهتين الذوق شعرك
poem0039_sa.translation_en: Do not compromise your noble character nor retreat, and do not let those lacking taste diminish your worth

poem0039_sa.text_ar: وأنت شعرك من عروق البرق راضع وزم روحك بين جنحانك ووكرك
poem0039_sa.translation_en: Your worth springs from the roots of lightning, so restrain your soul between your wings and nest

poem0039_sa.text_ar: كان ما عندك على النوماس مانع ولا تواضع للوضيع يضيع قدرك
poem0039_sa.translation_en: If you have nothing to offer people, do not humble yourself to the lowly lest they diminish your worth

poem0039_sa.text_ar: ما أنت مجبور على بعض التواضع
poem0039_sa.translation_en: You are not obligated to show humility to everyone

poem0038_sa.translation_en: The dark, abandoned road's journeys frighten me / But when I remember you, I say 'lengthen, oh my journeys'

poem0038_sa.text_ar: وانا لا من ذكرتك قلت طولي يا مشاويري
poem0038_sa.translation_en: My consolation is that you're my hope for forgiveness even if all paths are stumbling / Whether I arrive or not, I know you're not waiting for anyone but me, my love

poem0038_sa.text_ar: عزاي انك رجايا العذر لو كل الدروب عثور
poem0038_sa.translation_en: I swear by God, don't think I'm dull of feeling / Even if you're the impossible difficulty, I won't make excuses to you

poem0038_sa.text_ar: وصلت او ما وصلت اعرفك ما انت منتظر غيري حبيبي
poem0038_sa.translation_en: I love you despite the nights' wounding of my oppressed heart / Look how the wound grows while you grow in my thoughts

poem0038_sa.text_ar: وكّل الله لا تحسبني بليد الشعور
poem0038_sa.translation_en: May God place someone as a beloved for you / Who never becomes arrogant

poem0038_sa.text_ar: لو انت المستحيل الصعب ما أبدي لك معاذيري
poem0038_sa.translation_en: Why would I turn to people when your eyes are my audience / Welcome from your feet to your forehead to your scattered hair
poem0038_sa.imagery_tags: eyes as audience, physical beauty, scattered hair

poem0038_sa.text_ar: أحبك رغم تجريح الليالي لقلبي المقهور
poem0038_sa.translation_en: Welcome to everything in you, you're my most beautiful evenings / True love indeed mends the broken heart's ruins
No matter how much of my love I give you, I feel I'm falling short

poem0038_sa.text_ar: وسط تفكيري، أحد ربي يحطك له حبيب
poem0038_sa.translation_en: In the midst of my thoughts, may God place you there as a beloved for someone
And arrogance does not come to him

poem0038_sa.text_ar: أنا ليه التفت للناس وعيونك جماهيري
poem0038_sa.translation_en: Why would I turn to people when your eyes are my audience

poem0038_sa.text_ar: هلا بك من قدمك إلى غرتك
poem0038_sa.translation_en: Welcome to you from your feet to your forehead

poem0038_sa.text_ar: هلا في كل شي فيك وانت أحلى مساييري
poem0038_sa.translation_en: Welcome to everything in you, you are my sweetest evenings

poem0038_sa.text_ar: ترى صدق الغلا يجبر حطام الخاطر المكسور
poem0038_sa.translation_en: Indeed, true love mends the shattered fragments of a broken heart

poem0038_sa.text_ar: وأنا مهما منحتك من غلاي أشعر بتقصيري
poem0038_sa.translation_en: And no matter how much of my precious self I give you, I feel my shortcoming

poem_new_05.translation_en: I say she is arrogant
If description falls short, she violates it
With her eyelashes, she cuts the tongue of the poem
A figure that needs a topography specialist
To describe the runway of her waist to the platform
Arrogant
If description falls short, she violates it
With her eyelashes, she cuts the tongue of the poem

poem_new_05.text_ar: هيكل يبي عالم تضاريس مختص يوصف مدرج خصرها للمنصة
poem_new_05.translation_en: A figure needing a topography specialist to describe her waist's runway to the platform

poem_new_05.text_ar: متقسمة كل شيء خارج على النص
poem_new_05.translation_en: Divided, everything outside the script
She wronged a dress bound by its text

poem_new_05.text_ar: الكعب يوم الخصر من كتفه ارتص
poem_new_05.translation_en: The heel when the waist aligned from its shoulder
The hip aligned the leg and the leg aligned it

poem_new_05.text_ar: مشي المهار إيقاع وان عثرت وغصّ
poem_new_05.translation_en: The walk of young camels, rhythm, and if she stumbles and chokes

poem_new_05.text_ar: تك تك تك تك تك تك تك طار نصه
poem_new_05.translation_en: Tick tick tick tick tick tick tick, half flew away
The left leg slipped, compress compress
She limps and the chest of rhyme has a lump

poem_new_05.text_ar: أمزح مع حمدي على إيه بتبص
poem_new_05.translation_en: I joke with Hamdi about what you're looking at

poem_new_05.text_ar: يقول وانت تبص قلت إيه بصة
poem_new_05.translation_en: He says while you look, I said what a look
But it lasted long
But it lasted long, oh boy, my eye

poem_new_05.text_ar: وقول اص يفتح غطا الجفنين ويقول اصه
poem_new_05.translation_en: And say 'shh' to open the eyelids and say 'shh'
